ATA.led: No single country owns Antarctica. It is governed by the Antarctic Treaty, signed by 54 countries, which says it can only be used for peace and science.
ATA.langs: Whatever the scientists speak! English and Russian are most common at the research stations.
ATA.bfast: Whatever the research station chef makes. Hearty food like porridge, eggs, and toast to fuel a day in extreme cold.
ATA.big: Bigger than Europe. About 5.5 million square miles, but 98% is covered in ice.

IRN.led: A group of religious leaders is in charge, led by a Supreme Leader.
IRN.makes: Oil and gas. Iran has SO much oil underground that the whole world pays attention.
IRN.langs: Almost everyone speaks Farsi (also called Persian). One language, beautiful poetry.
IRN.gave: Algebra! The math you'll learn in school was invented by a Persian scientist.
IRN.bfast: Bread with feta cheese, walnuts, and sweet tea. Every single morning.
IRN.big: About the same size as Alaska. Really, really big.

NGA.led: The people vote for a president. Nigeria has had both elected leaders and military rulers.
NGA.makes: Oil. Nigeria makes more oil than almost any country in Africa.
NGA.langs: Over 500 languages! Most people speak English, Hausa, Yoruba, or Igbo.
NGA.gave: Afrobeats music. The whole world dances to it now.
NGA.bfast: Akara (fried bean cakes) and ogi (warm corn porridge). Crunchy and soft!
NGA.big: About twice the size of California.

GBR.led: There's a King, BUT the people also vote for a Prime Minister who runs things.
GBR.makes: Machines, medicine, and whisky. Scotland's whisky is famous everywhere.
GBR.langs: English, plus Scottish Gaelic and Welsh. Some words sound like magic spells.
GBR.gave: The telephone! Alexander Graham Bell was Scottish.
GBR.bfast: A 'full Scottish breakfast': eggs, bacon, sausage, beans, toast, and black pudding. Don't ask what's in the black pudding.
GBR.big: Scotland is about the size of South Carolina.

IRL.led: The people vote for a President and a Taoiseach (say TEE-shock), which is like a Prime Minister.
IRL.makes: Pharmaceuticals, technology, and dairy. Ireland exports more medicine per person than almost anywhere. Kerrygold butter goes worldwide.
IRL.langs: English and Irish (Gaelic). Irish is taught in every school but most people speak English day to day. Road signs are in both languages.
IRL.gave: Halloween! The ancient Celtic festival of Samhain became what you celebrate every October. Also Guinness, U2, and some of the greatest writers ever.
IRL.bfast: A full Irish breakfast: rashers (bacon), sausages, black and white pudding, eggs, toast, beans, and a strong cup of tea. Not coffee. TEA.
IRL.big: About the size of Indiana. A small island with an outsized impact on the world.

USA.led: The people vote for a President every four years.
USA.makes: Technology, airplanes, and movies. Hollywood makes films the whole world watches.
USA.langs: No official language, but almost everyone speaks English. Over 350 languages spoken!
USA.gave: The internet. And airplanes. And jazz music. And basketball.
USA.bfast: Cereal, pancakes, or eggs and bacon. YOU know this one!
USA.big: Third biggest country in the world. It takes 5 days to drive across it.

JPN.led: There's an Emperor, but the people vote for a Prime Minister who runs things.
JPN.makes: Cars, electronics, and robots. Toyota, Sony, and Nintendo are all Japanese.
JPN.langs: Almost everyone speaks Japanese. Just one language for 125 million people!
JPN.gave: Pokémon, Nintendo, emoji, and instant ramen noodles.
JPN.bfast: Rice, miso soup, grilled fish, and pickles. Yes, for BREAKFAST.

EGY.led: The people vote for a president, but the same leader has won for a long time.
EGY.makes: Cotton. Egyptian cotton is the softest, fanciest cotton in the world.
EGY.langs: Arabic. The writing goes from right to left, the opposite of English!
EGY.gave: Paper (papyrus), toothpaste, and the 365-day calendar we still use.
EGY.bfast: Ful medames: mashed beans with oil and lemon. Been eating it for thousands of years.
EGY.big: About the same size as Texas and New Mexico put together.

BRA.led: The people vote for a president. Brazil's government is only about 35 years old!
BRA.makes: Coffee! Brazil grows more coffee than any country on Earth. Also soybeans and sugar.
BRA.langs: Portuguese. NOT Spanish! Brazilians get annoyed if you mix them up.
BRA.gave: Bossa nova music, capoeira (a dance that's also a martial art), and the airplane. Yes, a Brazilian invented the airplane!
BRA.bfast: Pão de queijo (cheesy bread balls) and fresh tropical fruit juice.
BRA.big: Bigger than the entire United States without Alaska. HUGE.

MEX.led: The people vote for a president, but the president can only serve ONE term. Ever.
MEX.makes: Cars, electronics, and avocados. Almost every avocado you eat comes from Mexico.
MEX.langs: Spanish, plus 68 native languages that were here before Spanish arrived.
MEX.gave: Chocolate, chili peppers, corn, and tomatoes. Imagine pizza without tomatoes!
MEX.bfast: Chilaquiles: crispy tortilla chips covered in salsa and cheese. For BREAKFAST.
MEX.big: About three times the size of Texas.

AUS.led: The people vote for a Prime Minister. There's also a King (same one as England!).
AUS.makes: Iron ore and coal. Australia digs up rocks that get turned into buildings and bridges.
AUS.langs: English, plus over 250 Aboriginal languages that are thousands of years old.
AUS.gave: Wi-Fi! The technology that connects your iPad was invented in Australia.
AUS.bfast: Vegemite on toast. It's a dark, salty spread and Australians are OBSESSED.
AUS.big: Almost as big as the entire United States. It's a whole continent!

ITA.led: The people vote for a Prime Minister. Italy has had over 70 governments since World War II!
ITA.makes: Luxury goods: Ferrari cars, Gucci fashion, and the best olive oil on Earth.
ITA.langs: Italian. It sounds like someone singing even when they're just ordering lunch.
ITA.gave: Pizza, pasta, the piano, eyeglasses, and the battery that powers your toys.
ITA.bfast: A cornetto (like a croissant) and a cappuccino. That's it. Italians keep breakfast simple.

KEN.led: The people vote for a president. Kenya only became independent from Britain in 1963.
KEN.makes: Tea and coffee. Kenya is one of the biggest tea producers in the world.
KEN.langs: Swahili and English. 'Hakuna Matata' is REAL Swahili! It means 'no worries.'
KEN.gave: M-Pesa, a way to send money with your phone. Kenya invented mobile banking!
KEN.bfast: Ugali (thick corn porridge) with eggs and chai tea with milk.

FRA.led: The people vote for a president AND a prime minister. France has had FIVE different republics!
FRA.makes: Airplanes (Airbus), wine, cheese, and fancy perfume.
FRA.langs: French. It's spoken in 29 countries across the world, not just France!
FRA.gave: The hot air balloon, movies, braille (reading with your fingers), and the metric system.
FRA.bfast: Pain au chocolat and a bowl of hot chocolate. French kids dip their bread in it.

IND.led: The people vote for a Prime Minister. Biggest democracy on Earth!
IND.makes: Technology services, medicine, and spices. Indian IT workers help run companies worldwide.
IND.langs: 22 official languages! Hindi and English are the most common. Hundreds more exist.
IND.gave: The number zero, chess, yoga, and shampoo. The word 'shampoo' comes from Hindi!
IND.bfast: Dosa (crispy rice pancake) with chutney, or paratha bread with yogurt.
IND.big: About one-third the size of the United States, but with 4 TIMES as many people.

ISL.led: The people vote for a President and a Prime Minister. Iceland's parliament is 1,000 years old!
ISL.makes: Fish and geothermal energy. Iceland heats its buildings with HOT WATER from underground.
ISL.langs: Icelandic. It's barely changed in 1,000 years. Vikings would still understand it!
ISL.gave: The world's oldest parliament (Althing, year 930) and DNA research that helps cure diseases.
ISL.bfast: Skyr (thick, creamy yogurt) with berries and dark rye bread with butter.

CHN.led: One political party is in charge. The people do not vote for the top leader.
CHN.makes: Almost EVERYTHING. Phones, clothes, toys, electronics. Check your toys, many say 'Made in China.'
CHN.langs: Mandarin Chinese. More people speak it than any other language on Earth!
CHN.gave: Paper, gunpowder, the compass, printing, noodles, and fireworks.
CHN.bfast: Congee (warm rice porridge), steamed buns, and soy milk. Warm and cozy.
CHN.big: Almost the same size as the United States. Fourth biggest country in the world.

RUS.led: One president has been in charge for a very long time.
RUS.makes: Oil, gas, and wheat. Russia sends fuel and food to countries all over the world.
RUS.langs: Russian. The alphabet looks completely different — it's called Cyrillic!
RUS.gave: Tetris! The video game was invented by a Russian scientist.
RUS.bfast: Kasha (warm buckwheat porridge) with butter and jam.
RUS.big: Almost TWICE the size of the United States. The biggest country on Earth.

CAN.led: The people vote for a Prime Minister. There's also a King (same one as England).
CAN.makes: Oil, wood, and maple syrup. Canada's forests are bigger than most countries.
CAN.langs: English and French. In Quebec, everyone speaks French!
CAN.gave: Basketball! A Canadian teacher invented it in 1891.
CAN.bfast: Pancakes with real maple syrup. The REAL stuff, not the fake kind.
CAN.big: Second biggest country in the world, right above the United States.

DEU.led: The people vote for a Chancellor (that's like a prime minister).
DEU.makes: Cars! BMW, Mercedes, Volkswagen, Audi, and Porsche are ALL German.
DEU.langs: German. It has really long words. 'Donaudampfschifffahrt' is one word!
DEU.gave: The printing press, gummy bears, kindergarten, and Christmas trees.
DEU.bfast: Fresh bread rolls with butter, cheese, cold cuts, and a soft-boiled egg.

ESP.led: The people vote for a Prime Minister. There's also a King.
ESP.makes: Olive oil, wine, and oranges. Spain is the world's biggest olive oil producer.
ESP.langs: Spanish! It's spoken in over 20 countries. Also Catalan and Basque.
ESP.gave: The guitar, hot chocolate (thick like pudding), and the first global explorers.
ESP.bfast: Pan con tomate: toasted bread rubbed with tomato and olive oil. Simple and perfect.

KOR.led: The people vote for a president. South Korea went from one of the poorest countries to one of the richest in just 50 years.
KOR.makes: Electronics! Samsung phones, LG TVs, and Hyundai cars are all Korean.
KOR.langs: Korean. The alphabet (Hangul) was invented by a king to make reading easier!
KOR.gave: K-pop, Korean BBQ, and the fastest internet in the world.
KOR.bfast: Rice, soup, kimchi, and several small side dishes. Every meal is a feast.
KOR.big: About the size of Indiana. Small country, huge impact.

THA.led: There's a King, and the people also vote for a Prime Minister.
THA.makes: Rice! Thailand is one of the biggest rice exporters in the world. Also rubber.
THA.langs: Thai. The alphabet has 44 consonants and 15 vowels. Good luck!
THA.gave: Thai boxing (Muay Thai), pad thai, and the tuk-tuk (a tiny 3-wheeled taxi).
THA.bfast: Jok (warm rice porridge) with pork and a soft egg on top.
THA.big: About the size of California and Nevada combined.

TUR.led: The people vote for a president. Türkiye has shifted between military and civilian rule, and the current president has been in charge since 2014.
TUR.makes: Cars, clothes, and hazelnuts. Most of the world's hazelnuts come from Türkiye!
TUR.langs: Turkish. Plus Kurdish and several other languages.
TUR.gave: Tulips (the Dutch got them from Türkiye!), yogurt, and Turkish baths.
TUR.bfast: The BIGGEST breakfast you've ever seen: cheese, olives, tomatoes, cucumbers, honey, eggs, bread, jam, and tea. It takes up the whole table.

ZAF.led: The people vote for a President. Nelson Mandela was the first Black president.
ZAF.makes: Gold and diamonds. South Africa has some of the deepest mines on Earth.
ZAF.langs: ELEVEN official languages: Zulu, Xhosa, Afrikaans, English, and 7 more!
ZAF.gave: The CAT scan (hospital machine), the first heart transplant, and Rooibos tea.
ZAF.bfast: Pap (corn porridge) with milk, or rusks (hard biscuits) dipped in tea.
ZAF.big: About twice the size of Texas.

ARG.led: The people vote for a president. Argentina has had dramatic political swings, including a recent president who ran as a political outsider.
ARG.makes: Beef, soybeans, and wine. Argentinian steak is considered the best in the world.
ARG.langs: Spanish. Argentinians have a unique accent that sounds almost Italian!
ARG.gave: Tango dancing, dulce de leche (caramel spread), and some of the best soccer players ever.
ARG.bfast: Medialunas (sweet croissants) with café con leche (coffee with milk).
ARG.big: About the same size as India, but with way fewer people.

PER.led: The people vote for a president. Peru has had 6 presidents in just 5 years! Politics here move fast.
PER.makes: Copper, gold, and coffee. Peru's mines produce metals used in phones and computers.
PER.langs: Spanish, Quechua, and Aymara. Quechua was the language of the Inca Empire!
PER.gave: Potatoes! The world got potatoes from Peru. Also quinoa and ceviche.
PER.bfast: Pan con chicharrón (bread with crispy pork) and a sweet purple corn drink.
PER.big: About twice the size of Texas.

NOR.led: The people vote for a Prime Minister. There's also a King.
NOR.makes: Oil, gas, and fish. Norway's oil fund has saved over $1 TRILLION for the future.
NOR.langs: Norwegian. There are actually two written forms and nobody can agree which one to use!
NOR.gave: Skiing, the paper clip, and Viking exploration.
NOR.bfast: Bread with brunost (brown cheese) that tastes sweet and caramelly.
NOR.big: About the size of New Mexico, but really long and skinny.

GRC.led: The people vote for a Prime Minister and a President.
GRC.makes: Olive oil, feta cheese, and tourism. Millions visit the islands every year.
GRC.langs: Greek. The alphabet is different — Alpha, Beta, Gamma. That's where 'alphabet' comes from!
GRC.gave: Democracy, the Olympics, philosophy, geometry, and the alphabet concept.
GRC.bfast: Greek yogurt with honey and walnuts. Thick, creamy, and famous worldwide.

NZL.led: The people vote for a Prime Minister. There's also a King (same one as England).
NZL.makes: Dairy products, lamb, and wine. New Zealand butter and milk go all over the world.
NZL.langs: English, Māori, and New Zealand Sign Language. All three are official!
NZL.gave: Bungee jumping, the Haka dance, and special effects for Hollywood movies.
NZL.bfast: Marmite on toast (like Australia's Vegemite but different — don't mix them up!).

IDN.led: The people vote for a president. With 270 million people, Indonesia is the world's 3rd biggest democracy.
IDN.makes: Palm oil, rubber, and coffee. Indonesia is in SO many things you use every day.
IDN.gave: Batik fabric (wax-dyed patterns), shadow puppets, and instant noodles (Indomie!).
IDN.bfast: Nasi goreng: fried rice with egg and sweet soy sauce. For BREAKFAST!
IDN.big: Spread across the ocean, wider than the United States from coast to coast.

COL.led: The people vote for a President.
COL.makes: Coffee, flowers, and emeralds. Colombia sends more flowers to the US than any other country.
COL.langs: Spanish. Plus 65 indigenous languages still spoken in the Amazon region.
COL.gave: Shakira, salsa dancing, and the world's best emeralds.
COL.bfast: Arepa: a warm corn cake stuffed with cheese. Simple and delicious.
COL.big: About the size of Texas and California combined.

ARM.led: The people vote for a prime minister.
ARM.makes: Cognac (Armenian brandy is world-famous), copper, and diamonds.
ARM.langs: Armenian. It has its own completely unique alphabet created in 405 AD.
ARM.gave: The apricot (the Latin name literally means 'Armenian fruit'), one of the oldest alphabets still in use, and Armenian cognac.
ARM.bfast: Lahmajun (thin meat flatbread) with herbs and yogurt.

AZE.led: The same family has held the presidency since 1993.
AZE.makes: Oil and natural gas. Azerbaijan was one of the world's first oil-producing regions.
AZE.gave: One of the world's first oil wells, the Flame Towers of Baku, and more mud volcanoes than anywhere on Earth.
AZE.bfast: Qutab (stuffed flatbread) with herbs, or bread with cheese, butter, and tea.

BLR.led: The same president has been in charge since 1994. Belarus is often called 'Europe's last dictatorship.'
BLR.langs: Belarusian and Russian. Most people speak Russian day to day.
BLR.gave: Białowieża Forest (Europe's last primeval forest) and the rescue of the European bison from extinction.
BLR.bfast: Draniki (crispy potato pancakes) with sour cream.

BIH.led: Bosnia has a unique three-person presidency rotating between Bosniak, Croat, and Serb leaders.
BIH.langs: Bosnian, Croatian, and Serbian. Essentially the same language with three names.
BIH.gave: Stari Most bridge, the 1984 Winter Olympics (in Sarajevo), and one of the most complex political systems on Earth.
BIH.bfast: Burek (flaky meat pastry) with yogurt. The national breakfast obsession.
BIH.big: About the size of West Virginia.

BDI.led: The people vote for a president. Burundi has faced ethnic conflicts.
BDI.makes: Coffee and tea. Burundian coffee is gaining recognition.
BDI.langs: Kirundi and French. Swahili is also used.
BDI.gave: The Royal Drummers of Burundi (UNESCO cultural heritage) and some of the world's headwaters of the Nile.
BDI.bfast: Bread with tea, or beans with plantains.
BDI.big: About the size of Maryland. One of Africa's smallest.

LVA.led: The people vote for a president and parliament.
LVA.langs: Latvian. One of the oldest living Indo-European languages, related to Lithuanian.
LVA.gave: The Latvian Song and Dance Festival (UNESCO heritage) and Riga's stunning Art Nouveau architecture.
LVA.bfast: Rye bread with cottage cheese, or porridge with berries.
LVA.big: About the size of West Virginia.

MDA.led: The people vote for a president. Moldova is one of Europe's poorest countries but is reforming.
MDA.langs: Romanian (Moldovan). Russian is also widely spoken.
MDA.gave: The world's largest wine collection (Mileștii Mici, 200km of tunnels, 2 million bottles) and National Wine Day.
MDA.bfast: Plăcinte (stuffed pastries with cheese or fruit) and coffee.

MKD.led: The people vote for a president and prime minister. Changed its name from 'Macedonia' in 2019 after a dispute with Greece.
MKD.langs: Macedonian. Albanian is also official in some areas.
MKD.gave: Mother Teresa (born in Skopje), Lake Ohrid (UNESCO heritage), and one of the most creative city rebranding projects ever.
MKD.bfast: Burek with yogurt or bread with ajvar (roasted pepper spread).

MNE.led: The people vote for a president and prime minister. One of Europe's newest independent states.
MNE.makes: Tourism, aluminum, and agriculture. The coastline is Montenegro's biggest economic asset.
MNE.langs: Montenegrin (very similar to Serbian, Bosnian, and Croatian).
MNE.gave: Bay of Kotor (one of the most spectacular bays in the world) and Tara River Canyon (Europe's deepest).
MNE.bfast: Burek with yogurt or priganice (fried dough) with cheese and honey.

AND.led: TWO heads of state: the President of France AND a Spanish bishop.
AND.langs: Catalan. Spanish and French also used.
AND.gave: The only country with two foreign heads of state.
AND.bfast: Pa amb tomàquet (bread with tomato) and mountain cheese.
AND.big: About 2.5 times the size of Washington DC.

VAT.led: The Pope is the absolute ruler. A theocracy.
VAT.gave: Sistine Chapel, St. Peter's Basilica, Vatican Museums.
VAT.big: 0.17 square miles. Walk across it in 10 minutes.

MCO.led: A prince rules. The Grimaldi family has ruled for over 700 years.
MCO.makes: Tourism, finance, and gambling. No income tax.
MCO.gave: Monte Carlo Casino and Monaco Grand Prix.
MCO.bfast: French-style: croissant, tartine with jam, espresso.
MCO.big: About the size of Central Park in New York.

SMR.led: Two 'Captains Regent' serve as joint heads of state for just 6 months at a time.
SMR.makes: Tourism, ceramics, and postage stamps (collector items).
SMR.gave: The world's oldest surviving republic and a unique two-person rotating leadership.
SMR.big: About 24 square miles. You can drive across it in 15 minutes.

LIE.led: A prince rules with real political power.
LIE.makes: Precision instruments, dental products, and finance.
LIE.gave: The ability to rent an entire country for an event.
LIE.bfast: German/Swiss style: bread, cheese, cold cuts, coffee.

MLT.led: The people vote for a president and prime minister. Malta joined the EU in 2004.
MLT.makes: Tourism, online gaming, and financial services. Malta is a tech and gaming hub.
MLT.langs: Maltese and English. Maltese is the only Semitic language written in Latin script.
MLT.gave: Temples older than Stonehenge and the Pyramids, the Knights Hospitaller, and a unique language found nowhere else.
MLT.bfast: Pastizzi (flaky pastries with ricotta or peas) and tea or coffee.
MLT.big: About the size of Philadelphia. Tiny but packed with history.

BHS.led: The people vote for a prime minister. There's also a king (same one as England).
BHS.makes: Tourism, banking, and salt. Tourism is by far the biggest industry.
BHS.langs: English. Bahamian Creole is spoken informally.
BHS.gave: Swimming pigs, some of the clearest water on the planet, and Junkanoo (a vibrant street parade).
BHS.bfast: Boil fish with grits and johnny cake (slightly sweet bread).
BHS.big: About the size of Connecticut but spread across 700 islands.

BRB.led: Barbados became a republic in 2021, replacing the British monarch with a Barbadian president.
BRB.gave: Rihanna, Mount Gay Rum (the world's oldest rum brand), and a model for peacefully becoming a republic.
BRB.bfast: Bakes and saltfish, or breadfruit with fried fish.
BRB.big: About the size of San Antonio, Texas. One small island.

BLZ.led: The people vote for a prime minister. There's also a king (same one as England).
BLZ.makes: Sugar, bananas, and citrus. Tourism is growing fast thanks to the reef.
BLZ.langs: English! The only English-speaking country in Central America. Plus Spanish and Kriol.
BLZ.gave: The Great Blue Hole (one of the world's top dive sites), incredible Maya ruins, and a barrier reef second only to Australia's.
BLZ.bfast: Fry jacks (fried dough) with beans, eggs, and cheese.
BLZ.big: About the size of New Hampshire.

FJI.led: The people vote for a prime minister.
FJI.makes: Tourism, sugar, water (Fiji Water is from here!), and fish.
FJI.langs: English, Fijian, and Hindi. Fiji has a large Indian-heritage population.
FJI.gave: Fiji Water (from a aquifer on the island of Viti Levu), world-class rugby, and the word 'Bula' (hello/welcome).
FJI.bfast: Roti with curry or cassava with coconut milk.
FJI.big: About the size of New Jersey but spread across 330 islands in the South Pacific.

MDV.led: The people vote for a president. Climate change could sink the whole country.
MDV.makes: Tourism and fish. Almost entirely dependent on tourism.
MDV.langs: Dhivehi. English is widely spoken in tourism.
MDV.gave: Some of the most spectacular overwater bungalows, bioluminescent beaches, and a stark warning about climate change and rising seas.
MDV.bfast: Mas huni (tuna, coconut, and onion) with roshi flatbread and tea.
MDV.big: About 115 square miles of land spread across 1,200 islands. 99% is ocean.

BRN.led: A sultan has absolute power. The same family has ruled for over 600 years.
BRN.makes: Oil and natural gas. That's almost the entire economy.
BRN.langs: Malay. English and Chinese are also spoken.
BRN.gave: Istana Nurul Iman (the world's largest residential palace) and a model of an oil-wealthy micro-state.
BRN.bfast: Nasi katok (rice with fried chicken and sambal). Simple and beloved.

BTN.led: A king rules, but the people also vote for parliament. The king voluntarily gave up absolute power.
BTN.makes: Hydroelectric power (sold to India), tourism, and rice.
BTN.langs: Dzongkha. English is used in education.
BTN.gave: Gross National Happiness (the idea that a country should measure wellbeing, not just wealth), and a carbon-negative economy.
BTN.bfast: Red rice with ema datshi (chili and cheese stew). Spicy from the very first meal.
BTN.big: About the size of Switzerland. Hidden in the Himalayas.

KGZ.led: The people vote for a president. Kyrgyzstan has had several revolutions.
KGZ.gave: The World Nomad Games (Olympics for nomadic sports), eagle hunting traditions, and Issyk-Kul Lake.
KGZ.bfast: Bread with kaymak (clotted cream) and honey, plus tea with milk.
KGZ.big: About the size of South Dakota.

TLS.led: The people vote for a president and prime minister. One of the youngest nations on Earth.
TLS.makes: Coffee and oil. Timor-Leste's economy is heavily dependent on oil revenues.
TLS.langs: Tetum and Portuguese are official. Over 30 indigenous languages.
TLS.gave: Some of the most pristine and unexplored coral reefs left on Earth.
TLS.bfast: Batar daan (corn and mung bean porridge) or bread with coffee.

SYC.led: The people vote for a president.
SYC.makes: Tourism and fishing. One of Africa's richest countries per person.
SYC.langs: Seychellois Creole, English, and French. All three are official.
SYC.gave: Some of the most photographed beaches on Earth and the Aldabra giant tortoise.
SYC.bfast: Breadfruit chips with fish curry or bread with jam and tea.
SYC.big: 115 islands with a total land area about the size of Washington DC.

MUS.led: The people vote for a prime minister and president.
MUS.makes: Sugar, textiles, and tourism. Mauritius is one of Africa's most developed economies.
MUS.langs: English is official. French and Mauritian Creole are most widely spoken. Hindi and others too.
MUS.gave: The dodo bird (now sadly extinct, but a powerful symbol of conservation), and the concept of 'underwater waterfall.'
MUS.bfast: Dholl puri (flatbread with ground lentils) with curry and chutney.
MUS.big: About the size of Jacksonville, Florida. One small island in the Indian Ocean.

COM.led: The presidency rotates between the three main islands.
COM.langs: Comorian, Arabic, and French are all official.
COM.gave: Ylang-ylang essence (used in Chanel No. 5 and other luxury perfumes) and the coelacanth (a 'living fossil' fish thought to be extinct for 65 million years).
COM.bfast: Breadfruit with coconut milk or rice with fish.
COM.big: About the size of Luxemburg. Four tiny islands.

STP.led: The people vote for a president.
STP.makes: Cocoa and coffee. São Toméan chocolate is gaining premium recognition.
STP.langs: Portuguese. Plus Forro and other Creoles.
STP.gave: Some of the world's finest single-origin chocolate and pristine tropical forests.
STP.bfast: Bread with butter and local coffee, or calulu (fish stew with vegetables).
STP.big: About the size of New York City. Two tiny islands.

CPV.led: The people vote for a president and prime minister. Cabo Verde is one of Africa's most stable democracies.
CPV.makes: Fish, tourism, and remittances from Cabo Verdeans living abroad.
CPV.gave: Morna music (UNESCO heritage), Cesária Évora, and a model for democracy in Africa.
CPV.bfast: Cachupa (slow-cooked stew of corn, beans, and fish). The national dish, eaten morning and night.
CPV.big: 10 volcanic islands with a total area about the size of Rhode Island.

KNA.led: The people vote for a prime minister. There's also a king (UK monarch).
KNA.makes: Tourism and citizenship-by-investment (you can buy citizenship).
KNA.gave: The first Caribbean island colonized by Europeans and one of the smallest sovereign nations.
KNA.big: About 100 square miles total. Two tiny islands.

LCA.led: The people vote for a prime minister.
LCA.gave: Two Nobel Prize winners from a population of only 180,000, the Pitons, and a drive-in volcano.
LCA.bfast: Bakes with saltfish and cocoa tea.
LCA.big: About 238 square miles. One small Caribbean island.

VCT.led: The people vote for a prime minister.
VCT.gave: The filming location for Pirates of the Caribbean, spectacular sailing, and La Soufrière volcano.
VCT.bfast: Roasted breadfruit with saltfish and fried plantains.
VCT.big: 32 islands with about 150 square miles total.

DMA.led: The people vote for a president and prime minister.
DMA.makes: Agriculture and ecotourism. Bananas and coconut.
DMA.gave: The Boiling Lake and pristine Caribbean rainforest.
DMA.bfast: Bakes with saltfish or plantains with cocoa tea.

GRD.led: The people vote for a prime minister.
GRD.gave: Underwater Sculpture Park and world-class nutmeg.
GRD.bfast: Oil down (breadfruit and coconut milk stew).

ATG.led: The people vote for a prime minister. There's also a king (UK monarch).
ATG.gave: 365 beaches (one for each day), Nelson's Dockyard, and the Antigua Sailing Week.
ATG.bfast: Saltfish and chop-up (vegetables) with bread or fungie (cornmeal).
ATG.big: About 170 square miles. Two main islands.

WSM.led: A mix of traditional chief system and parliamentary democracy.
WSM.gave: The fire knife dance, the concept of fa'a Samoa (one of the world's last intact traditional cultures), and incredible rugby talent.
WSM.bfast: Koko Samoa (Samoan cocoa drink) with bread or taro.
WSM.big: About the size of Rhode Island.

SLB.led: The people vote for a prime minister.
SLB.langs: English is official. Solomon Islands Pijin is common. 70+ indigenous languages.
SLB.gave: Guadalcanal WWII battle site and extraordinary marine biodiversity.
SLB.bfast: Cassava or sweet potato with coconut cream.
SLB.big: Nearly 1,000 islands in the South Pacific.

VUT.led: The people vote for prime minister and president.
VUT.langs: Bislama, English, and French. Plus 100+ indigenous languages.
VUT.gave: Land diving (original bungee jumping) and kava culture.
VUT.bfast: Laplap (grated root vegetable baked with coconut cream).

TON.led: A king is in charge with parliament. Only Pacific monarchy.
TON.gave: The only Pacific kingdom never colonized.
TON.bfast: Lu (taro leaves with coconut cream baked underground).

PLW.led: The people vote for a president.
PLW.gave: Jellyfish Lake, world's first shark sanctuary.
PLW.bfast: Fish with taro or rice and coconut.

MHL.led: The people vote for a president. Free association with the US.
MHL.big: 70 square miles across 750,000 square miles of ocean.

FSM.led: The people vote for a president. Free association with the US.
FSM.makes: Fish and copra. Dependent on fishing licenses and US support.
FSM.langs: English plus Chuukese, Pohnpeian, Yapese, and Kosraean.
FSM.gave: Nan Madol (Venice of the Pacific) and Yap's giant stone money.
FSM.big: 607 islands, only 271 square miles of land.

NRU.makes: Very little now. Phosphate is largely depleted.
NRU.gave: A cautionary tale about using up all natural resources.
NRU.big: About 8 square miles. One tiny oval island.

KIR.led: The people vote for a president.
KIR.makes: Fish and copra. Fishing licenses are the main income.
KIR.gave: First place on Earth to see each new day.
KIR.bfast: Rice with fish, or pandanus fruit and coconut.
KIR.big: 33 atolls across 1.3 million square miles of ocean.

TUV.led: Parliament elected. King (UK monarch) is head of state.
TUV.makes: Almost nothing. Income from .tv domain and fishing licenses.
TUV.gave: The .tv internet domain and a warning about sea level rise.
TUV.bfast: Pulaka (swamp taro) or breadfruit with fish.
TUV.big: About 10 square miles. 9 tiny coral atolls.

XKX.led: The people vote for a president and prime minister. Not all countries recognize Kosovo's independence.
XKX.makes: Mining, textiles, and agriculture. The economy is young and growing.
XKX.langs: Albanian and Serbian are both official.
XKX.gave: Bear Sanctuary Pristina (rescuing bears from captivity) and one of Europe's youngest, most dynamic populations.
XKX.bfast: Flija (layered crepe-like pastry) or burek with yogurt.

DZA.led: The people vote for a president. The same leader often stays for decades.
DZA.makes: Oil and natural gas. Algeria is one of the biggest gas exporters in the world.
DZA.langs: Arabic and Berber (Amazigh). French is also widely spoken.
DZA.gave: Couscous, Saharan rock art that's 8,000 years old, and one of the oldest universities in the world.
DZA.bfast: Khobz (crusty bread) with olive oil, jam, and strong coffee.
DZA.big: Biggest in Africa, 10th biggest in the world. 80% is Sahara Desert.

AGO.led: The people vote for a president. The same party has been in charge since 1975.
AGO.makes: Oil and diamonds. Angola is one of Africa's biggest oil producers.
AGO.langs: Portuguese (from colonial history) plus Umbundu, Kimbundu, and Kikongo.
AGO.gave: Kuduro music and dance, which spread across the Portuguese-speaking world.
AGO.bfast: Bread with butter and coffee, or funge (cassava porridge) with fish.
AGO.big: About twice the size of Texas.

CMR.led: The same president has been in charge since 1982. One of the longest-ruling leaders in the world.
CMR.makes: Oil, cocoa, and coffee. Cameroon is a major cocoa producer.
CMR.langs: French AND English are both official. One of few countries with both!
CMR.gave: Roger Milla's iconic corner-flag dance at the 1990 World Cup.
CMR.bfast: Beignets (fried dough) with beans and pap (corn porridge).

COD.led: The people vote for a president. DR Congo has faced many conflicts.
COD.makes: Cobalt, copper, and coltan. The minerals in your PHONE probably came from here.
COD.langs: French plus over 200 local languages! Lingala, Swahili, Tshiluba, and Kikongo are the biggest.
COD.gave: Lingala music (soukous/rumba) that influenced music across all of Africa.
COD.bfast: Fufu (pounded cassava) with fish stew or bread with tea.
COD.big: About the size of Western Europe combined. Third biggest country in Africa.

GHA.led: The people vote for a president. Ghana is one of Africa's strongest democracies.
GHA.makes: Cocoa (for chocolate), gold, and oil. Ghana is called the 'Gold Coast.'
GHA.langs: English plus over 80 local languages. Akan and Twi are the most common.
GHA.gave: Kente cloth (colorful woven fabric worn by royalty), highlife music, and fantasy coffins shaped like anything you can imagine.
GHA.bfast: Waakye (rice and beans) with fried plantains and spicy shito sauce.

SDN.led: Military leaders have often been in charge. Sudan has faced internal conflicts.
SDN.makes: Gold, oil, and gum arabic (used in candy and soda). Sudan produces most of the world's gum arabic.
SDN.langs: Arabic and English are official. Plus over 100 other languages.
SDN.gave: Ancient Nubian civilization, which rivaled Egypt. And the pyramids of Meroë.
SDN.bfast: Ful medames (mashed fava beans) with bread, cheese, and sweet tea.
SDN.big: Third biggest country in Africa. About one-quarter the size of the US.

SEN.led: The people vote for a president. Senegal is one of Africa's most stable democracies.
SEN.makes: Fish, peanuts, and phosphates. Senegal exports fish all over West Africa.
SEN.langs: French is official, but Wolof is what everyone actually speaks day to day.
SEN.gave: Teranga (the Senegalese concept of extreme hospitality), Youssou N'Dour's music, and thiéboudienne (the national dish).
SEN.bfast: Bread with chocolate spread and café Touba (spiced coffee). French bakery influence is strong.
SEN.big: About the size of South Dakota.

SOM.led: Somalia has struggled with conflict and different groups controlling different areas.
SOM.makes: Livestock (camels, goats, sheep) and bananas. Somali camels are exported across the Middle East.
SOM.langs: Somali and Arabic. Somali wasn't written down until 1972!
SOM.gave: Frankincense and myrrh (the gifts from the Christmas story came from this region), and an incredible oral poetry tradition.
SOM.bfast: Canjeero (spongy pancake like Ethiopian injera) with tea and sugar.

MOZ.led: The people vote for a president. A democracy since 1994.
MOZ.makes: Aluminum, coal, and shrimp. Mozambique's prawns are famous across southern Africa.
MOZ.langs: Portuguese, plus Makhuwa, Changana, and dozens more.
MOZ.gave: Marrabenta music (Mozambique's signature sound) and some of the best seafood in Africa.
MOZ.bfast: Bread with butter and tea, or xima (corn porridge) with a stew.
MOZ.big: About twice the size of California.

MDG.led: The people vote for a president. Madagascar has had political instability and coups.
MDG.makes: Vanilla! Madagascar produces most of the world's vanilla. Also cloves, coffee, and gems.
MDG.langs: Malagasy and French. Malagasy is related to languages from Indonesia, not Africa!
MDG.gave: Over 100 species of lemur (found nowhere else), vanilla flavoring, and unique baobab forests.
MDG.bfast: Mofo gasy (sweet rice cakes) with coffee. Street vendors sell them fresh every morning.
MDG.big: 4th largest island in the world. About the size of Texas.

UGA.led: The same president has been in charge since 1986.
UGA.makes: Coffee, tea, and fish. Ugandan coffee is gaining a reputation worldwide.
UGA.langs: English and Swahili are official. Luganda is the most spoken local language.
UGA.gave: Winston Churchill called it 'the Pearl of Africa.' Also home to half the world's mountain gorillas.
UGA.bfast: Rolex! (Not the watch. It's a chapati rolled around an omelet. Seriously.)
UGA.big: About the size of Oregon. Landlocked, but has enormous lakes.

ZMB.led: The people vote for a president. Zambia has had peaceful transfers of power.
ZMB.makes: Copper. Zambia is one of the biggest copper producers in the world.
ZMB.langs: English is official. Plus over 70 local languages including Bemba, Nyanja, and Tonga.
ZMB.gave: Victoria Falls (shared with Zimbabwe), some of the world's best walking safaris, and the emerald trade.
ZMB.bfast: Nshima (thick corn porridge) with relish, or bread with tea.

ZWE.led: The people vote for a president. Zimbabwe has had political challenges.
ZWE.makes: Platinum, gold, and tobacco. Zimbabwe has some of Africa's richest mineral deposits.
ZWE.langs: 16 official languages! English, Shona, and Ndebele are the most common.
ZWE.gave: Great Zimbabwe (one of Africa's most impressive ancient cities), and the stone bird that's a national symbol.
ZWE.bfast: Sadza (thick porridge) with peanut butter, or bread with tea.

NER.led: Niger has experienced several military coups, most recently in 2023.
NER.makes: Uranium (used for nuclear power), gold, and livestock.
NER.langs: French is official. Hausa and Zarma are the most spoken.
NER.gave: Ancient Saharan rock art, and the Cure Salée (Festival of the Nomads) where thousands gather in the desert.
NER.bfast: Fura (millet porridge balls) soaked in milk. Simple desert food.
NER.big: About twice the size of Texas. Mostly desert.

MLI.led: Military leaders have taken power multiple times, most recently in 2020.
MLI.makes: Gold, cotton, and livestock. Mali is one of Africa's biggest gold producers.
MLI.langs: French is official. Bambara is the most widely spoken.
MLI.gave: The blues! The musical roots of American blues can be traced to Malian musicians. Also Timbuktu's manuscripts.
MLI.bfast: Dégué (millet porridge with yogurt) or bread with butter and Nescafé.
MLI.big: About twice the size of Texas. Much of it is Sahara.

TCD.led: The same family has been in charge for decades. Chad has faced many conflicts.
TCD.makes: Oil, cotton, and livestock. Oil was discovered in the 2000s.
TCD.langs: French and Arabic are official. Plus over 120 local languages!
TCD.gave: Some of the oldest rock art in Africa, and Zakouma National Park's incredible elephant conservation success.
TCD.bfast: Kisra (fermented sorghum flatbread) with a stew or sauce.
TCD.big: About twice the size of Texas. A huge, landlocked country.

IRQ.led: The people vote for a president and prime minister. Iraq has faced wars and is rebuilding.
IRQ.makes: Oil. Iraq has some of the largest oil reserves in the world.
IRQ.gave: Writing, the wheel, mathematics, the concept of time (60 minutes in an hour), and the earliest known laws.
IRQ.bfast: Khubz (flatbread) with eggs, cheese, cream, and sweet tea.

ISR.led: The people vote for a prime minister. Israel's politics are complex and constantly debated worldwide.
ISR.makes: Technology, diamonds (cutting and polishing), and agricultural innovation.
ISR.langs: Hebrew and Arabic. Hebrew was actually revived from an ancient language!
ISR.gave: Cherry tomatoes, the USB flash drive, drip irrigation, and Waze navigation app.
ISR.bfast: Israeli breakfast is legendary: hummus, salad, eggs, bread, cheese, and olives.
ISR.big: About the size of New Jersey. Very small but very significant.

JOR.led: A king is in charge. Jordan's royal family is respected for maintaining stability.
JOR.makes: Potash and phosphates (used in fertilizer), and a growing tech sector.
JOR.langs: Arabic. Most educated Jordanians also speak English.
JOR.gave: Petra (one of the New Seven Wonders of the World) and mansaf (a lamb dish considered the national treasure).
JOR.bfast: Hummus, falafel, bread, and sweet tea. Sometimes with labneh (thick yogurt).

SYR.led: Syria has been in a devastating civil war since 2011 that has displaced millions.
SYR.makes: Historically: oil, textiles, and agriculture. The war has disrupted the economy severely.
SYR.langs: Arabic. Kurdish, Armenian, and other languages are also spoken.
SYR.gave: The alphabet (it evolved from ancient Syrian scripts), soap (Aleppo soap is 4,000 years old), and Damascus steel.
SYR.bfast: Fatayer (stuffed pastries), labneh with olive oil, and mint tea.
SYR.big: About the size of North Dakota.

YEM.led: Yemen has been in a civil war since 2014.
YEM.makes: Historically: coffee, oil, and fish. The conflict has devastated the economy.
YEM.gave: The global coffee trade (the word 'mocha' comes from the Yemeni port of Mocha), and frankincense.
YEM.bfast: Fattah (bread soaked in broth with eggs and honey) and bint al-sahn (honey cake).
YEM.big: About the size of California and Nevada combined.

OMN.led: A sultan is in charge. Oman has been led by sultans for centuries.
OMN.makes: Oil, natural gas, and fish. Oman is diversifying into tourism.
OMN.langs: Arabic. English is widely used in business.
OMN.gave: Frankincense (one of the ancient world's most valuable substances), and some of the earliest maritime trade routes.
OMN.bfast: Shuwa (slow-cooked meat) with bread and kahwa (Omani coffee with cardamom).

ARE.led: Each of the 7 emirates has its own ruler. Together they're led by a president.
ARE.makes: Oil, tourism, trade, and finance. Dubai is a global hub for business.
ARE.langs: Arabic. English is so widely used that some areas feel more English than Arabic.
ARE.gave: Burj Khalifa, the world's busiest international airport, and a model for rapid development.
ARE.bfast: Balaleet (sweet vermicelli with eggs) and karak chai (strong spiced tea).
ARE.big: About the size of Maine. 7 emirates including Dubai and Abu Dhabi.

QAT.led: An emir (like a king) is in charge. The Al Thani family has ruled for over 150 years.
QAT.makes: Natural gas and oil. Qatar has some of the world's largest natural gas reserves.
QAT.gave: Al Jazeera news network and some of the most ambitious architecture projects in the world.
QAT.bfast: Balaleet (sweet noodles) with eggs and strong karak tea.
QAT.big: About the size of Connecticut. One of the smallest countries in the world.

KWT.led: An emir is in charge, with an elected parliament.
KWT.makes: Oil and petrochemicals. Kuwait's economy runs almost entirely on oil.
KWT.langs: Arabic. English is widely taught and used.
KWT.gave: One of the first modern welfare states in the Middle East, and the world's largest recorded fireworks display.
KWT.bfast: Chebab (saffron pancakes) with date syrup, cheese, and tea.
KWT.big: About the size of Hawaii. Tiny but wealthy.

LBN.led: The people vote for a president and prime minister. Lebanon's political system divides power among religious groups.
LBN.makes: Banking, tourism, and agriculture. Lebanon was once called the 'Switzerland of the Middle East.'
LBN.langs: Arabic. French and English are also widely spoken. Most Lebanese speak 3 languages.
LBN.gave: The Phoenician alphabet (which became the basis for Greek, Latin, and YOUR alphabet), hummus, and tabbouleh.
LBN.bfast: Manoushe: flatbread with za'atar (herb mix) and olive oil. The national breakfast.
LBN.big: About the size of Connecticut. One of the smallest countries in the Middle East.

AFG.led: The Taliban took control in 2021 after US forces withdrew.
AFG.makes: Dried fruits, nuts, and carpets. Afghan carpets are prized worldwide.
AFG.langs: Dari (Persian) and Pashto are both official.
AFG.gave: Beautiful handwoven carpets, lapis lazuli gemstones (used in ancient Egyptian jewelry), and buzkashi (a sport played on horseback with a goat).
AFG.bfast: Bread with tea, cream, and sometimes eggs. Bread is sacred in Afghan culture.

MMR.led: The military took control in a 2021 coup. There is ongoing resistance.
MMR.makes: Natural gas, jade, and rubies. Myanmar's jade is the finest in the world.
MMR.gave: Some of the world's finest rubies and jade, Bagan's temple complex, and Burmese cats.
MMR.bfast: Mohinga: rice noodles in rich fish broth. THE national dish, eaten for breakfast everywhere.

NPL.led: Nepal became a republic in 2008 after being a monarchy for 240 years.
NPL.makes: Tourism, carpets, and tea. Everest climbers spend millions here.
NPL.langs: Nepali is official. Plus over 120 other languages spoken in the mountains and valleys.
NPL.gave: Mount Everest, the birthplace of Buddha (Lumbini), and the Gurkha soldiers known as the bravest warriors.
NPL.bfast: Dal bhat (lentils and rice). Eaten twice a day, every day. There's a saying: 'Dal bhat power, 24 hour.'
NPL.big: About the size of Arkansas. But vertically, from lowlands to the highest point on Earth.

LKA.led: The people vote for a president. Sri Lanka faced a major economic crisis in 2022.
LKA.makes: Tea (Ceylon tea is world-famous), rubber, and cinnamon. Sri Lanka was the world's largest cinnamon producer.
LKA.langs: Sinhala and Tamil are both official. English is widely used.
LKA.gave: Ceylon tea, cinnamon (the original source), and ancient irrigation systems that were engineering marvels.
LKA.bfast: String hoppers (steamed rice noodle nests) with coconut sambol and curry. Light and delicious.
LKA.big: About the size of West Virginia. A small island with huge diversity.

MYS.led: There's a unique rotating monarchy. Nine sultans take turns being king every 5 years!
MYS.makes: Electronics, palm oil, and rubber. Malaysia is one of the world's biggest palm oil producers.
MYS.langs: Malay (Bahasa Malaysia), plus Chinese, Tamil, and English.
MYS.gave: Petronas Towers, the rotating monarchy system, and some of the world's best street food.
MYS.bfast: Nasi lemak: coconut rice with sambal, peanuts, egg, and anchovies. The national dish.
MYS.big: About the size of New Mexico. Split into two parts separated by the South China Sea.

MNG.led: The people vote for a president. Mongolia is a democracy squeezed between Russia and China.
MNG.makes: Coal, copper, and cashmere. Mongolian cashmere comes from their enormous goat herds.
MNG.langs: Mongolian. Written in Cyrillic script (like Russian).
MNG.gave: The largest contiguous empire in history (under Genghis Khan), and the tradition of eagle hunting.
MNG.bfast: Suutei tsai (salty milk tea) with boortsog (fried dough) and dried curds.
MNG.big: About the size of Alaska. The most sparsely populated country on Earth.

KHM.led: There's a king, but the prime minister runs the government. The same party has been in charge since the 1990s.
KHM.makes: Textiles (clothing), rice, and tourism. Angkor Wat brings millions of visitors.
KHM.langs: Khmer. The Khmer alphabet has the most letters of any alphabet in the world (74)!
KHM.gave: Angkor Wat (the largest religious monument ever built) and the Khmer Empire's incredible engineering.
KHM.bfast: Bai sach chrouk: pork and rice with pickled vegetables. Simple and perfect.

LAO.led: One party has been in charge since 1975.
LAO.makes: Hydroelectric power (from the Mekong River), copper, and gold.
LAO.langs: Lao. Very similar to Thai. Speakers of both can mostly understand each other.
LAO.gave: Luang Prabang (one of Southeast Asia's most beautiful towns), and the tradition of morning alms-giving.
LAO.bfast: Khao piak sen (chicken noodle soup). Comforting and warm.
LAO.big: About the size of Utah. Landlocked, surrounded by 5 countries.

UZB.led: The people vote for a president. Uzbekistan has been opening up to the world after decades of isolation.
UZB.makes: Cotton, gold, and natural gas. Uzbekistan is one of the world's biggest cotton producers.
UZB.langs: Uzbek. Russian is also widely spoken.
UZB.gave: Samarkand's stunning architecture, the Silk Road trading tradition, and plov (pilaf) culture.
UZB.bfast: Non (flatbread) with tea, cream, and sometimes plov leftovers from dinner.

TJK.led: The same president has been in charge since 1994.
TJK.makes: Aluminum, cotton, and hydroelectric power from all those mountain rivers.
TJK.langs: Tajik (very similar to Persian/Farsi). Russian is also widely used.
TJK.gave: Some of the most dramatic mountain scenery on Earth and the Pamir Highway.
TJK.bfast: Non (flatbread) with butter, honey, and tea. Simple mountain food.
TJK.big: About the size of Wisconsin. Almost entirely mountains.

TKM.led: One party controls everything. Turkmenistan is one of the most isolated countries on Earth.
TKM.makes: Natural gas. Turkmenistan has some of the world's largest gas reserves.
TKM.gave: Akhal-Teke horses (possibly the most beautiful horse breed on Earth) and the Darvaza gas crater.
TKM.bfast: Chorek (flatbread) with tea and kaymak (thick cream).
TKM.big: About the size of California. Mostly Karakum Desert.

SGP.led: The people vote for a parliament, but one party has won every election since independence.
SGP.makes: Electronics, finance, and shipping. Singapore's port is one of the busiest in the world.
SGP.langs: English, Mandarin, Malay, and Tamil. FOUR official languages! 'Singlish' mixes them all.
SGP.gave: Gardens by the Bay, Changi Airport (voted world's best repeatedly), and a model for clean, efficient city-living.
SGP.bfast: Kaya toast: bread with coconut jam and butter, plus a soft-boiled egg and strong coffee.
SGP.big: About the size of San Antonio, Texas. You can drive across the whole country in an hour.

TWN.led: The people vote for a president. Taiwan considers itself independent, but China claims it.
TWN.makes: Semiconductor chips, electronics, and machinery. TSMC chips power most of the world's devices.
TWN.langs: Mandarin Chinese, Taiwanese Hokkien, and indigenous languages.
TWN.gave: Bubble tea (boba), the world's most advanced computer chips, and some of the best street food in Asia.
TWN.bfast: Dan bing (egg crepe) with soy milk. Found at every street corner.
TWN.big: About the size of Maryland. Small island, massive economic impact.

PRK.led: The Kim family has ruled since 1948. Citizens cannot freely leave the country.
PRK.makes: Coal, minerals, and military equipment. The economy is heavily controlled by the government.
PRK.langs: Korean. Same language as South Korea, but some words have diverged.
PRK.gave: Very little reaches the outside world due to extreme isolation.
PRK.bfast: Rice porridge or noodles with kimchi, though food can be scarce.

PRT.led: The people vote for a president and prime minister.
PRT.makes: Cork (Portugal produces half the world's cork!), wine, and textiles.
PRT.langs: Portuguese. Spoken in 9 countries because of Portugal's exploration history.
PRT.gave: The Age of Exploration, tempura (brought to Japan!), custard tarts, and half the world's cork.
PRT.bfast: Pastéis de nata with a galão (milky coffee). Bakeries open early everywhere.
PRT.big: About the size of Indiana. The westernmost country in mainland Europe.

AUT.led: The people vote for a president and chancellor.
AUT.makes: Machinery, cars, and tourism. The Alps bring millions of skiers.
AUT.gave: Mozart, Strauss waltzes, the waltz dance itself, psychoanalysis (Freud), and Red Bull.
AUT.bfast: Semmel (bread roll) with butter and jam, a soft-boiled egg, and Viennese coffee.

CHE.led: The people have MORE power than anywhere. They vote on almost everything through referendums.
CHE.makes: Watches, chocolate, cheese, pharmaceuticals, and banking.
CHE.langs: FOUR official languages: German, French, Italian, and Romansh. Depends which part you're in.
CHE.gave: The Red Cross, Swiss Army knives, LSD (discovered by accident), and the World Wide Web (CERN is here).
CHE.bfast: Birchermüesli (the original overnight oats, invented here) with bread, cheese, and coffee.
CHE.big: About twice the size of New Jersey.

BEL.led: The people vote for a prime minister. There's also a king.
BEL.makes: Chocolate, beer, diamonds (Antwerp is the diamond capital), and pharmaceuticals.
BEL.langs: Dutch (in the north), French (in the south), and German. The country is literally split in half by language!
BEL.gave: Belgian waffles, French fries (actually Belgian!), the saxophone, and some of the world's finest beer.
BEL.bfast: Bread with chocolate spread, cheese, and cold cuts. Or a fresh waffle from a street vendor.
BEL.big: About the size of Maryland. Tiny but mighty.

DNK.led: The people vote for a prime minister. There's also a queen (now a king as of 2024).
DNK.makes: Wind turbines, pharmaceuticals, and shipping (Maersk is the world's biggest shipping company).
DNK.langs: Danish. Almost everyone speaks perfect English too.
DNK.gave: LEGO, Hans Christian Andersen's fairy tales (The Little Mermaid, The Ugly Duckling), and the concept of hygge.
DNK.bfast: Rugbrød (dark rye bread) with butter and toppings like cheese, herring, or liver pâté.
DNK.big: About the size of Massachusetts and New Hampshire combined.

FIN.led: The people vote for a president and prime minister.
FIN.makes: Electronics (Nokia), paper, and machinery. Finland has one of the most educated workforces in the world.
FIN.langs: Finnish and Swedish. Finnish is one of the hardest languages to learn, unrelated to almost every other European language.
FIN.gave: Nokia phones, Linux (the operating system), Santa Claus's official home, and one of the world's best education systems.
FIN.bfast: Porridge with berries, rye bread with butter and cheese, and strong coffee. Finns drink MORE coffee per person than anyone.
FIN.big: About the size of New Mexico. Lots of lakes (188,000 of them!).

CZE.led: The people vote for a president and prime minister.
CZE.langs: Czech. A Slavic language with lots of consonant clusters (try saying 'zmrzlina' — that means ice cream).
CZE.gave: The word 'robot' (from a 1920 play), contact lenses, sugar cubes, and Pilsner beer.
CZE.bfast: Rohlik (bread rolls) with butter, cheese, and ham. Plus strong coffee.
CZE.big: About the size of South Carolina.

HUN.led: The people vote for a prime minister and president.
HUN.makes: Cars, electronics, and pharmaceuticals. Many European car brands have factories here.
HUN.langs: Hungarian (Magyar). Unrelated to any neighboring language. One of Europe's hardest languages.
HUN.gave: The Rubik's Cube, ballpoint pen, vitamin C discovery, and goulash.
HUN.bfast: Bread with butter and paprika-spiced lard, plus strong coffee.

ROU.led: The people vote for a president and prime minister.
ROU.makes: Cars (Dacia), IT services, and agriculture. Romania has a booming tech sector.
ROU.langs: Romanian. It's a Romance language, closer to Italian and French than to neighboring Slavic languages.
ROU.gave: Dracula (Bram Stoker was inspired by Romanian prince Vlad the Impaler), insulin discovery, and the jet engine.
ROU.bfast: Bread with butter, cheese, and tomatoes. Or covrigi (Romanian pretzels) from a street vendor.

SRB.led: The people vote for a president and prime minister.
SRB.makes: Cars, agriculture, and IT services. Serbia's tech scene is growing fast.
SRB.langs: Serbian. Written in BOTH Cyrillic AND Latin alphabets. One of few countries that uses both!
SRB.gave: Nikola Tesla (AC electricity), Novak Djokovic (tennis champion), and rakija (fruit brandy).
SRB.bfast: Burek (flaky pastry with cheese or meat) and yogurt. The classic Balkan breakfast.
SRB.big: About the size of South Carolina.

HRV.led: The people vote for a president and prime minister.
HRV.langs: Croatian. Very similar to Serbian and Bosnian.
HRV.gave: The necktie (cravat comes from 'Croat'), the torpedo, and Dubrovnik.
HRV.bfast: Bread with prosciutto, cheese, and olive oil. Mediterranean simplicity.
HRV.big: About the size of West Virginia.

BGR.led: The people vote for a president and prime minister.
BGR.makes: Rose oil, machinery, and agriculture. Bulgarian rose oil is in many luxury perfumes.
BGR.langs: Bulgarian. Uses the Cyrillic alphabet, which was actually created here!
BGR.gave: The Cyrillic alphabet (originally developed in Bulgaria), rose oil, and Bulgarian yogurt (with unique bacteria).
BGR.bfast: Banitsa (filo pastry with cheese) and boza (a fermented wheat drink). Traditional and filling.

SVK.led: The people vote for a president and prime minister.
SVK.makes: Cars! Slovakia produces more cars per capita than any country on Earth (Volkswagen, Kia, Peugeot).
SVK.langs: Slovak. Very similar to Czech. Speakers can mostly understand each other.
SVK.gave: Andy Warhol's parents were Slovak, and the country has over 180 castles and castle ruins.
SVK.bfast: Bread with butter and local cheese, or bryndzové halušky (potato dumplings with sheep cheese).
SVK.big: About the size of New Hampshire and Vermont combined.

SVN.led: The people vote for a president and prime minister.
SVN.makes: Pharmaceuticals, machinery, and tourism. Slovenia is one of the most developed former Yugoslav countries.
SVN.langs: Slovenian. Despite being tiny, it has 46 distinct dialects!
SVN.gave: Lake Bled (one of the most photographed places in Europe), and Melania Trump (though she became a US citizen).
SVN.bfast: Bread with honey and butter, or potica (nut roll pastry) with coffee.
SVN.big: About the size of New Jersey.

EST.led: The people vote for a president and prime minister.
EST.makes: Technology, electronics, and IT services. Estonia's e-government is a model for the world.
EST.langs: Estonian. Related to Finnish, not to neighboring Russian or Latvian.
EST.gave: Skype (video calling was invented here), e-residency (digital citizenship), and one of the world's first fully digital governments.
EST.bfast: Dark rye bread with butter and cheese, plus a soft-boiled egg and coffee.
EST.big: About the size of New Hampshire and Vermont combined.

LTU.led: The people vote for a president and parliament.
LTU.makes: Lasers! Lithuania makes some of the world's most advanced laser technology.
LTU.langs: Lithuanian. One of the oldest Indo-European languages still spoken, barely changed for centuries.
LTU.gave: Some of the world's best laser technology, and Vilnius (a beautiful baroque capital).
LTU.bfast: Šaltibarščiai (cold pink beet soup) in summer, or bread with cheese and coffee.
LTU.big: About the size of West Virginia.

LUX.led: A grand duke is in charge (the only grand duchy left in the world). There's also an elected parliament.
LUX.makes: Steel, finance, and satellite communications. Home to many EU institutions.
LUX.langs: Luxembourgish, French, AND German. All three are official. Most people speak all three plus English!
LUX.gave: The European Union (many EU institutions are headquartered here) and Skype (co-founded by a Luxembourger).
LUX.bfast: Bread with butter, cheese, and cold cuts. German and French breakfast traditions merged.
LUX.big: About the size of Rhode Island. One of the smallest countries in the world.

CYP.led: The people vote for a president. The island is divided between Greek and Turkish sides since 1974.
CYP.langs: Greek and Turkish (the island is divided). English is very widely spoken.
CYP.gave: Halloumi cheese, the legend of Aphrodite (goddess of love), and commandaria wine (possibly the world's oldest named wine).
CYP.bfast: Halloumi with eggs, tomatoes, and bread. Or a village breakfast with olives and fresh cheese.
CYP.big: About the size of Connecticut. Third largest island in the Mediterranean.

ALB.led: The people vote for a prime minister and president.
ALB.makes: Textiles, footwear, and chrome. Albania's economy is growing fast.
ALB.langs: Albanian. An ancient language that's its own branch of Indo-European, not closely related to any neighbor.
ALB.gave: Mother Teresa (born to an Albanian family), bunkers everywhere (700,000 were built during isolation), and stunning coastline.
ALB.bfast: Byrek (filo pastry with cheese or spinach) and Turkish-style coffee.

GEO.led: The people vote for a prime minister and president.
GEO.makes: Wine, manganese, and agriculture. Georgian wine made in clay pots (qvevri) is UNESCO-recognized.
GEO.langs: Georgian. One of the oldest alphabets in the world, used since the 5th century.
GEO.gave: Wine (the earliest evidence of winemaking, 8,000 years ago), khachapuri (cheese bread), and one of the world's oldest and most unique alphabets.
GEO.bfast: Khachapuri: boat-shaped bread filled with melted cheese, butter, and a raw egg stirred in. Unreal.
GEO.big: About the size of West Virginia.

VEN.led: A president is in charge but the country has faced political crisis and economic collapse.
VEN.makes: Oil. Venezuela has the largest proven oil reserves in the world, but the economy has collapsed.
VEN.langs: Spanish. Plus indigenous languages in the south.
VEN.gave: Angel Falls, more Miss Universe winners than any other country, and the birthplace of Simón Bolívar who liberated much of South America.
VEN.bfast: Arepa: a corn cake split open and stuffed with cheese, beans, or meat. The national food.
VEN.big: About the size of Texas and Oklahoma combined.

ECU.led: The people vote for a president. Ecuador has had 20+ presidents since 1996!
ECU.makes: Oil, bananas, and shrimp. Ecuador is the world's biggest banana exporter.
ECU.langs: Spanish. Plus Quechua and other indigenous languages.
ECU.gave: The Galápagos Islands (which inspired Darwin's theory of evolution), panama hats (actually made in Ecuador!), and cacao.
ECU.bfast: Bolón de verde: a ball of mashed plantain stuffed with cheese or pork. Hearty and filling.

BOL.led: The people vote for a president. Bolivia has its first indigenous president in history.
BOL.makes: Natural gas, zinc, and tin. Also quinoa, which has become popular worldwide.
BOL.langs: Spanish plus 36 official indigenous languages! Quechua and Aymara are the most spoken.
BOL.gave: Quinoa (the superfood), Salar de Uyuni (the world's most incredible natural mirror), and some of the oldest civilizations in the Americas.
BOL.bfast: Salteña: a juicy baked empanada with meat, potatoes, and spicy sauce inside.
BOL.big: About the size of Texas and California combined.

PRY.led: The people vote for a president. Had a dictator for 35 years straight.
PRY.makes: Soybeans, hydroelectric power, and beef. The Itaipú Dam was once the world's largest.
PRY.langs: Spanish AND Guaraní are both official. Paraguay is one of the most bilingual countries on Earth.
PRY.gave: Guaraní language preservation (one of the few indigenous languages spoken by the majority of a country), and yerba mate tea.
PRY.bfast: Chipa (cheesy bread balls) with cocido (burnt mate tea with milk).
PRY.big: About the size of California. Landlocked in the heart of South America.

URY.led: The people vote for a president. Uruguay is one of the strongest democracies in the Americas.
URY.makes: Beef, soybeans, and wool. Uruguayan beef is some of the best in the world.
URY.langs: Spanish. With a distinctive accent that sounds a bit like Italian.
URY.gave: The first FIFA World Cup, tango (shared with Argentina), and one of the first countries to legalize many progressive policies.
URY.bfast: Mate tea (shared from one cup with a metal straw) with bizcochos (pastries).
URY.big: About the size of Washington state.

GUY.led: The people vote for a president. A huge oil discovery is changing everything.
GUY.makes: Oil (recently discovered), gold, and sugar. A massive oil discovery is transforming Guyana's economy.
GUY.langs: English! The only English-speaking country in South America. Plus Creole and indigenous languages.
GUY.gave: Kaieteur Falls (one of the world's most powerful waterfalls) and one of the most pristine rainforests left.
GUY.bfast: Roti with curry, or bake and saltfish. Caribbean and Indian food traditions mix.

SUR.led: The people vote for a president. One of the most diverse countries on Earth.
SUR.makes: Gold, oil, and bauxite (used to make aluminum).
SUR.langs: Dutch! The only Dutch-speaking country in South America. Plus Sranan Tongo (Creole) and many others.
SUR.gave: One of the most ethnically diverse countries in the world, where Diwali, Eid, and Christmas are all national holidays.
SUR.bfast: Broodje bakkeljauw (bread with salted cod) or roti with curry.
SUR.big: About the size of Georgia (the US state). Smallest in South America.

GTM.led: The people vote for a president. Heart of the ancient Maya civilization.
GTM.makes: Coffee, sugar, and bananas. Guatemalan coffee is considered among the world's finest.
GTM.langs: Spanish. Plus 21 Mayan languages still spoken!
GTM.gave: Chocolate (the Maya invented drinking chocolate), the marimba instrument, and Tikal.
GTM.bfast: Desayuno chapín: eggs, beans, plantains, tortillas, and cheese. A full spread.

HND.led: The people vote for a president. Elected its first female president in 2022.
HND.makes: Coffee, bananas, and palm oil. Honduras is Central America's biggest coffee exporter.
HND.langs: Spanish. Plus Garifuna and indigenous languages on the coast.
HND.gave: Copán (one of the most important Maya archaeological sites) and the Garifuna culture.
HND.bfast: Baleada: a flour tortilla with beans, cheese, and cream. The national street food.

NIC.led: The same president has been in charge for many years, with increasing control.
NIC.makes: Coffee, beef, and gold. Nicaraguan coffee is excellent.
NIC.langs: Spanish. Plus Miskito and English Creole on the Caribbean coast.
NIC.gave: Rubén Darío (considered the father of modern Spanish poetry) and volcano boarding.
NIC.bfast: Gallo pinto: rice and beans fried together with onions. The national dish.
NIC.big: About the size of New York state. Biggest in Central America.

SLV.led: The people vote for a president. The current president made Bitcoin legal tender in 2021.
SLV.gave: Pupusas (thick corn tortillas stuffed with cheese, beans, or pork) and the Bitcoin adoption experiment.
SLV.bfast: Pupusas with curtido (pickled cabbage slaw) and coffee. Yes, pupusas for breakfast.
SLV.big: About the size of Massachusetts. Smallest in Central America.

CRI.led: The people vote for a president. Costa Rica is one of the most stable democracies in Latin America.
CRI.makes: Computer chips, bananas, and coffee. Also a LOT of renewable energy.
CRI.gave: A model for environmental conservation, 'Pura Vida' philosophy, and proof that a country doesn't need a military.
CRI.bfast: Gallo pinto (rice and beans) with eggs, plantains, and sour cream.
CRI.big: About the size of West Virginia.

PAN.led: The people vote for a president. The Canal earns billions in fees.
PAN.makes: Canal fees, banking, and shipping. The Canal earns billions in fees.
PAN.gave: The Panama Canal (one of the greatest engineering achievements ever), and the crossroads between North and South America.
PAN.bfast: Hojaldras (fried dough) with eggs and cheese.
PAN.big: About the size of South Carolina.

JAM.led: The people vote for a prime minister. There's also a king (same one as England).
JAM.makes: Bauxite (for aluminum), coffee, and tourism. Blue Mountain coffee is legendary.
JAM.langs: English, plus Jamaican Patois (Creole) which is what everyone actually speaks.
JAM.gave: Reggae music, Bob Marley, Usain Bolt, dancehall music, and jerk seasoning.
JAM.bfast: Ackee and saltfish: a fruit that looks like scrambled eggs, cooked with salted cod. The national dish.
JAM.big: About the size of Connecticut. Small island, massive cultural impact.

DOM.led: The people vote for a president. Baseball is more popular than politics.
DOM.makes: Gold, cigars, and tourism. Resorts bring millions of visitors.
DOM.gave: More MLB players than any non-US country, merengue and bachata music, and Santo Domingo (the first permanent European settlement in the Americas).
DOM.bfast: Mangú: mashed plantains with fried eggs, salami, and fried cheese. Filling!
DOM.big: About the size of Vermont and New Hampshire combined. Shares the island of Hispaniola with Haiti.

HTI.led: The people vote for a president, but Haiti has faced extreme political instability and gang violence.
HTI.makes: Textiles, mangoes, and cocoa. Haiti's economy faces severe challenges.
HTI.langs: Haitian Creole and French. Almost everyone speaks Creole; French is used in schools and government.
HTI.gave: The first successful slave revolution in history, vibrant art and music traditions, and the concept that ALL people deserve freedom.
HTI.bfast: Spaghetti with hot dogs and ketchup (yes, really!) or bread with peanut butter and coffee.
HTI.big: About the size of Maryland. Shares the island of Hispaniola with the Dominican Republic.

TTO.led: The people vote for a prime minister and president.
TTO.makes: Oil, natural gas, and petrochemicals. Also asphalt from a natural tar lake.
TTO.langs: English, plus Trinidad Creole and Tobagonian Creole.
TTO.gave: The steel pan (steel drum), calypso and soca music, limbo dancing, and Carnival.
TTO.bfast: Doubles: fried bread with curried chickpeas and hot pepper sauce. The ultimate street food.
TTO.big: About the size of Delaware. Two islands.

PNG.led: The people vote for a prime minister. Papua New Guinea gained independence from Australia in 1975.
PNG.makes: Gold, copper, oil, and coffee. Mining is the biggest industry.
PNG.langs: OVER 800 LANGUAGES. Most linguistically diverse country on Earth! Tok Pisin and English are official.
PNG.gave: The most linguistic diversity on Earth, incredible tribal art and masks, and some of the most biodiverse forests remaining.
PNG.bfast: Mumu (earth oven-cooked food) or sweet potato and greens.
PNG.big: About the size of California. Eastern half of a large island, plus hundreds of smaller islands.

DJI.led: The same president has been in charge since 1999.
DJI.makes: Services and port operations. Djibouti's port is a major shipping hub for the region.
DJI.langs: French and Arabic are official. Somali and Afar are widely spoken.
DJI.gave: A strategic location at the mouth of the Red Sea that's been important for trade for thousands of years.
DJI.bfast: Canjeero (flatbread) with butter and tea. Simple and filling.
DJI.big: About the size of New Hampshire.

ERI.led: One party has been in charge since independence in 1993. No elections have been held.
ERI.langs: No official language, but Tigrinya, Arabic, and English are widely used.
ERI.gave: Asmara's stunning Modernist architecture (UNESCO World Heritage), and incredible resilience.
ERI.bfast: Injera with a spicy lentil stew and tea.

GAB.led: The military took control in 2023 after the same family ruled for over 50 years.
GAB.langs: French. Plus Fang and other Bantu languages.
GAB.gave: One of Africa's best-protected rainforests and Lopé National Park (UNESCO World Heritage).
GAB.bfast: Baguette with butter and coffee (French influence) or plantains with sauce.

COG.led: The same president has been in charge since 1997.
COG.gave: Some of the best-preserved lowland gorilla habitat on Earth.
COG.bfast: Baguette with butter and coffee, or saka-saka (cassava leaf stew) leftovers.

CAF.led: The country has faced conflict and instability. UN peacekeepers are present.
CAF.makes: Diamonds, timber, and cotton. Rich in resources but development is limited.
CAF.gave: Dzanga-Sangha: one of the last places to see forest elephants and western lowland gorillas in the wild.
CAF.bfast: Cassava porridge with greens or bread with tea.
CAF.big: About the size of Texas. Right in the middle of the continent.

SSD.led: South Sudan has faced civil war since shortly after independence.
SSD.makes: Oil is the main export. The economy is heavily dependent on it.
SSD.langs: English is official. Plus Arabic, Dinka, Nuer, and many others.
SSD.gave: The joy of a new nation being born, and the world's second-largest wildlife migration (after the Serengeti).
SSD.bfast: Kisra (fermented sorghum bread) with stew, or porridge.

RWA.led: The same president has led since 2000. Rwanda has the highest percentage of women in parliament worldwide.
RWA.makes: Coffee, tea, and tin. Rwanda's economy is growing rapidly.
RWA.langs: Kinyarwanda, English, and French are all official.
RWA.gave: A model for post-conflict recovery, gorilla conservation, and the highest percentage of women in parliament in the world (over 60%).
RWA.bfast: Igisafuriya (one-pot stew) with bread, or porridge with fruit.
RWA.big: About the size of Maryland. One of the smallest countries in Africa.

MWI.led: The people vote for a president. Had its first female president in 2012.
MWI.makes: Tobacco, tea, and sugar. Agriculture is the backbone of the economy.
MWI.gave: Lake Malawi (a UNESCO World Heritage site with over 1,000 fish species) and incredible warmth and hospitality.
MWI.bfast: Nsima (thick porridge) with chambo (fish) or vegetables.

SLE.led: The people vote for a president. Rebuilding after civil war and Ebola.
SLE.langs: English is official. Krio (Creole English) is the most spoken.
SLE.gave: Some of the world's largest diamonds, and incredible resilience through civil war and Ebola.
SLE.bfast: Rice and groundnut soup, or bread with fried fish and tea.
SLE.big: About the size of South Carolina.

LBR.led: The people vote for a president. Liberia elected Africa's first female head of state (Ellen Johnson Sirleaf) in 2006.
LBR.makes: Rubber, iron ore, and timber. Also has one of the world's largest ship registries.
LBR.langs: English (the only African country founded by Americans where English is the main language). Plus 20+ local languages.
LBR.gave: Africa's first female president, a unique connection to American history, and a symbol of freedom.
LBR.bfast: Farina (cassava porridge) or bread with fried plantains and coffee.

GIN.makes: Bauxite (for aluminum), gold, and diamonds. Guinea has the world's largest bauxite reserves.
GIN.langs: French is official. Plus Fula, Malinke, Susu, and others.
GIN.gave: The djembe drum, which is now used in music around the entire world.
GIN.bfast: Rice porridge or bread with butter and Nescafé.

TGO.led: The same family has been in power since 1967.
TGO.gave: Koutammakou (UNESCO World Heritage mud tower houses) and a window into West African voodoo traditions.
TGO.bfast: Bread with butter and coffee, or akume (corn paste) with sauce.
TGO.big: About the size of West Virginia. Long and narrow.

BEN.led: The people vote for a president, though opposition has been restricted.
BEN.makes: Cotton, cashew nuts, and shea butter.
BEN.langs: French. Plus Fon, Yoruba, and others.
BEN.gave: Voodoo religion (which spread to Haiti and the Americas), the Dahomey Amazons (female warriors), and Ganvié (the 'Venice of Africa' on stilts).
BEN.bfast: Aklui (corn porridge with milk) or bread with omelets.

GNQ.led: The same president has been in charge since 1979, making him one of the longest-ruling leaders in the world.
GNQ.makes: Oil and natural gas. Almost entirely dependent on oil revenue.
GNQ.langs: Spanish! The only country in Africa where Spanish is official. Also French and Portuguese.
GNQ.gave: A unique cultural bridge between Africa, Spain, and Latin America.
GNQ.bfast: Baguette with butter and coffee (Spanish and French colonial influence).
GNQ.big: About the size of Maryland. Mainland plus several islands.

NAM.led: The people vote for a president. Namibia gained independence from South Africa in 1990.
NAM.makes: Diamonds, uranium, and fish. Namibia is the world's 4th largest uranium producer.
NAM.langs: English is official. Plus Afrikaans, Oshiwambo, and German (from colonial history).
NAM.gave: Some of the most dramatic landscapes on Earth, desert-adapted elephants, and Sossusvlei's iconic red dunes.
NAM.bfast: Bread with butter and jam, or porridge. German-style baked goods are also popular (from colonial influence).
NAM.big: About the size of Texas and Louisiana combined.

BWA.led: The people vote for a president. Botswana is one of Africa's most stable and successful democracies.
BWA.makes: Diamonds! Botswana is the world's largest diamond producer by value.
BWA.gave: A model for how a poor country can develop responsibly, the Okavango Delta (one of the world's greatest wildlife destinations), and diamond prosperity.
BWA.bfast: Ting (sorghum porridge) with milk and sugar, or bread with tea.

SWZ.led: A king has absolute power. This is Africa's last absolute monarchy.
SWZ.makes: Sugar, soft drink concentrates, and textiles.
SWZ.gave: The Reed Dance ceremony (one of Africa's largest cultural events) and the name change from Swaziland to Eswatini in 2018.
SWZ.bfast: Porridge with sour milk or bread with tea.
SWZ.big: About the size of New Jersey. One of Africa's smallest countries.

LSO.led: There's a king, but the prime minister runs the government.
LSO.makes: Water (sold to South Africa), diamonds, and textiles.
LSO.gave: The Basotho blanket tradition and proof that Africa has mountains, snow, and even a ski resort.
LSO.bfast: Papa (thick maize porridge) with milk or bread and tea.
LSO.big: About the size of Maryland. Completely surrounded by South Africa.

MRT.led: Military coups have been common. Currently has an elected president.
MRT.langs: Arabic. French and local languages are also spoken.
MRT.gave: The Eye of the Sahara (a geological mystery) and ancient cities buried in the sand.
MRT.bfast: Bread with butter and tea (Mauritanian tea ceremony involves 3 rounds of progressively sweeter tea).
MRT.big: About the size of Texas and New Mexico combined. Mostly desert.

GMB.led: The people vote for a president. A long-ruling dictator was voted out in 2017.
GMB.makes: Peanuts, fish, and tourism. Peanuts are the main cash crop.
GMB.langs: English. Plus Mandinka, Wolof, Fula, and others.
GMB.gave: A unique position as a sliver of a country inside Senegal, and some of West Africa's best beach tourism.
GMB.bfast: Bread with butter and attaya (green tea ceremony).
GMB.big: About the size of a thin strip. 300 miles long but only 20 miles wide in most places.

GNB.led: Political instability and coups have been common.
GNB.makes: Cashew nuts. Guinea-Bissau is one of the world's biggest cashew exporters.
GNB.langs: Portuguese. Plus Kriol (Creole) which everyone speaks.
GNB.gave: The Bijagós Islands (a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve with unique saltwater hippos).
GNB.bfast: Bread with butter and coffee, or rice with fish sauce.

TUN.led: The people vote for a president. Tunisia sparked the Arab Spring democracy movement in 2011.
TUN.makes: Olive oil, phosphates, and textiles. Tunisia is a major olive oil producer.
TUN.langs: Arabic. French is widely used in business and education.
TUN.gave: Ancient Carthage (rival to Rome), the spark that started the Arab Spring, and Tatooine from Star Wars (named after the town Tataouine).
TUN.bfast: Lablabi (chickpea soup with bread) or baguette with harissa (chili paste).
TUN.big: About the size of Georgia (US state).

LBY.led: Libya has been in conflict since 2011 when Gaddafi was overthrown. Multiple groups control different areas.
LBY.makes: Oil and natural gas. Almost entirely dependent on oil.
LBY.langs: Arabic. Berber languages are also spoken.
LBY.gave: Leptis Magna (one of the best-preserved Roman cities on Earth) and some of the Sahara's most dramatic landscapes.
LBY.bfast: Bazeen (barley bread) with lamb sauce, or bread with olive oil.
LBY.big: About the size of Alaska. 4th biggest country in Africa.

NCL.led: A French territory with increasing self-governance. Independence referendums were held recently.
