NCL.makes: Nickel. New Caledonia has about 25% of the world's known nickel reserves.
NCL.gave: One of the world's most spectacular lagoon and reef systems (UNESCO World Heritage).
NCL.bfast: Baguette and coffee (French influence) or bougna (traditional wrapped food).
NCL.big: About the size of New Jersey.

PRI.led: A US territory. Puerto Ricans are US citizens but can't vote for president.
PRI.makes: Pharmaceuticals, medical devices, and rum. Bacardí rum is from here.
PRI.langs: Spanish and English are both official.
PRI.gave: Reggaetón, Bad Bunny, piña coladas, and bioluminescent bays that glow in the dark.
PRI.bfast: Mallorca (sweet bread sandwich) with ham and cheese, and café con leche.
PRI.big: About the size of Connecticut. An island in the Caribbean.

BFA.led: Military took control in two coups in 2022.
BFA.makes: Gold, cotton, and shea butter (used in cosmetics worldwide).
BFA.langs: French is official. Plus Moore, Dioula, and many others.
BFA.gave: FESPACO (Africa's biggest film festival), Thomas Sankara (the revolutionary leader called 'Africa's Che Guevara'), and shea butter.
BFA.bfast: Tô (millet or corn porridge) with sauce or bread with coffee.

CIV.led: The people vote for a president.
CIV.makes: Cocoa (the world's biggest producer), coffee, and rubber.
CIV.langs: French. Plus over 60 local languages including Baoulé and Dioula.
CIV.gave: Nearly half the world's cocoa supply, Didier Drogba (soccer legend), and coupé-décalé music.
CIV.bfast: Aloco (fried plantains) with chili sauce and fish, or bread with café au lait.
CIV.big: About the size of New Mexico.

SWE.led: The people vote for a Prime Minister. There's also a King and Queen, but they're mostly ceremonial.
SWE.makes: Cars (Volvo), furniture (IKEA), music, and technology. Sweden punches way above its weight.
SWE.langs: Swedish. Almost everyone also speaks perfect English.
SWE.gave: Minecraft, Spotify, IKEA, the Nobel Prize, and dynamite (yes, the same guy invented both).
SWE.bfast: Open-face sandwiches with cheese, cucumber, and caviar from a tube. Yes, a TUBE.
SWE.big: About the size of California. Long and skinny, stretching way up toward the Arctic.

NLD.led: The people vote for a Prime Minister. There's also a King.
NLD.makes: Flowers (they export BILLIONS of tulips), cheese, and high-tech machinery.
NLD.langs: Dutch. It sounds like a mix of English and German. Most Dutch people speak 3-4 languages.
NLD.gave: The microscope, the stock market, orange carrots (they were bred orange on purpose!), and WiFi routers.
NLD.bfast: Hagelslag: chocolate sprinkles on buttered bread. For BREAKFAST. It's completely normal here.
NLD.big: About twice the size of New Jersey. Tiny but mighty.

PHL.led: The people vote for a President.
PHL.makes: Electronics, coconut products, and bananas. The Philippines is one of the world's biggest banana exporters.
PHL.langs: Filipino (Tagalog) and English. Most people switch between both in the same sentence!
PHL.gave: Karaoke machines, the yo-yo, and jeepneys (the most colorfully decorated buses you've ever seen).
PHL.bfast: Tapsilog: sweet beef jerky, garlic fried rice, and a fried egg. Breakfast of champions.
PHL.big: About the size of Arizona, but spread across 7,600 islands.

BGD.led: The people vote for a Prime Minister.
BGD.makes: Clothing! Look at the labels in your closet. There's a good chance something says 'Made in Bangladesh.'
BGD.langs: Bengali (Bangla). People fought a war to protect their language. February 21 is International Mother Language Day because of Bangladesh.
BGD.gave: Muslin fabric (the finest cloth in ancient history), microcredit banking, and the Grameen Bank that helps poor people start businesses.
BGD.bfast: Paratha with dal (lentil curry) and a fried egg. Filling and spicy.
BGD.big: About the size of Iowa, but with 170 MILLION people squeezed in.

TZA.led: The people vote for a president. Tanzania was formed when two countries (Tanganyika and Zanzibar) merged in 1964.
TZA.makes: Gold, coffee, and spices. Zanzibar produces most of the world's cloves.
TZA.langs: Swahili and English. Swahili was the first African language to be used as an internet domain (.sw).
TZA.gave: Olduvai Gorge, where some of the oldest human fossils on Earth were discovered. We might ALL come from Tanzania.
TZA.bfast: Chapati (flatbread) with beans and chai tea. Simple and filling.
TZA.big: About twice the size of California. Kilimanjaro is 19,341 feet tall.

KAZ.led: The same president was in charge for almost 30 years. A new one took over in 2019.
KAZ.makes: Oil, metals, and uranium. Kazakhstan has more uranium than almost anywhere on Earth.
KAZ.langs: Kazakh and Russian. Most people speak both.
KAZ.gave: Apples! The first apples in history grew in Kazakhstan. The old capital is even called Almaty, which means 'full of apples.'
KAZ.bfast: Baursak (puffy fried bread) with butter and jam, plus chai tea with milk.
KAZ.big: 9th biggest country in the world. Bigger than all of Western Europe combined.

SAU.led: A king is in charge. Saudi Arabia is named after the royal family, the House of Saud.
SAU.makes: Oil. Saudi Arabia has so much oil underground that it changed the entire world's economy.
SAU.langs: Arabic. It's written from right to left and has beautiful calligraphy.
SAU.gave: Arabic numerals (1, 2, 3...), the numbers YOU use every day, came through the Arab world.
SAU.bfast: Ful (mashed beans), flatbread, dates, and Arabic coffee with cardamom.
SAU.big: About one-quarter the size of the United States. Mostly sand.

ETH.led: The people vote for a prime minister. Ethiopia is one of the only African countries never colonized by Europe.
ETH.makes: Coffee, flowers, and sesame seeds. Ethiopian coffee ceremonies can take 3 hours.
ETH.langs: Amharic (with its own beautiful alphabet), plus over 80 other languages!
ETH.gave: Coffee! The entire world's coffee obsession started with Ethiopian goats.
ETH.bfast: Injera (spongy sourdough flatbread) with scrambled eggs and spicy sauce.
ETH.big: About twice the size of Texas. Over 120 million people live here.

MAR.led: A king is in charge. Morocco's royal family has ruled for over 350 years.
MAR.makes: Phosphates (used in fertilizer), citrus fruits, and argan oil for fancy shampoo.
MAR.langs: Arabic and Berber (Amazigh). French is also widely spoken.
MAR.gave: Leather goods, couscous, and the fez hat that you've definitely seen in old movies.
MAR.bfast: Msemen (flaky flatbread) with honey and butter, plus sweet mint tea.
MAR.big: About the size of California. Right across from Spain, separated by just 9 miles of water.

VNM.led: One political party is in charge, similar to China.
VNM.makes: Electronics, clothing, and rice. Vietnam is the world's 2nd biggest coffee exporter!
VNM.langs: Vietnamese. It uses the same alphabet as English but with lots of accent marks.
VNM.gave: Pho soup, spring rolls, and ao dai (a beautiful traditional dress).
VNM.bfast: Pho! Hot noodle soup with herbs and chili. Yes, soup for breakfast. It's perfect.
VNM.big: About the size of New Mexico. Long and skinny, like a stretched S.

POL.led: The people vote for a president AND a prime minister.
POL.makes: Furniture, car parts, and electronics. Poland's economy is one of the fastest-growing in Europe.
POL.langs: Polish. It has sounds that English speakers can't even pronounce. Try 'szczęście' (happiness).
POL.gave: Marie Curie (discovered radioactivity), Copernicus (proved Earth orbits the Sun), and pierogi.
POL.bfast: Bread with butter and ham, a soft-boiled egg, and tea. Simple and filling.
POL.big: About the size of New Mexico. Right in the middle of Europe.

UKR.led: The people vote for a president. Ukraine has been defending itself in a war since 2022.
UKR.makes: Wheat, sunflower oil, and steel. Ukraine's farms help feed millions of people worldwide.
UKR.langs: Ukrainian. It uses the Cyrillic alphabet, like Russian, but it's a completely different language.
UKR.gave: Borscht (beet soup that's bright PINK), pysanky eggs, and some of the world's best farmland.
UKR.bfast: Syrniki (sweet cheese pancakes) with sour cream and jam.
UKR.big: The biggest country entirely in Europe. About the size of Texas.

CUB.led: One party has been in charge since 1959. The Castro family led for over 60 years.
CUB.makes: Sugar, tobacco (for cigars), and nickel. Cuba's cigars are the most famous in the world.
CUB.langs: Spanish. Cuban Spanish is fast and musical, with its own slang.
CUB.gave: The mojito cocktail, rumba dancing, and some of the world's best baseball players.
CUB.bfast: Tostada (toasted bread) with butter and café con leche (coffee with milk).
CUB.big: The biggest island in the Caribbean. About the size of Tennessee.

CHL.led: The people vote for a president. Chile is one of the most stable democracies in South America.
CHL.makes: Copper! Chile produces more copper than any country on Earth. Also wine and salmon.
CHL.langs: Spanish. Chilean Spanish is so fast and full of slang that other Spanish speakers struggle to understand it.
CHL.gave: Easter Island (with the giant stone head statues), Pablo Neruda's poetry, and the world's best telescopes.
CHL.bfast: Bread with avocado (called 'palta') and a cup of tea. Avocado toast before it was cool.
CHL.big: 2,700 miles long but only 110 miles wide. Like a ribbon down the side of the continent.

PAK.led: The people vote for a prime minister, but the military has also been in charge at times.
PAK.makes: Textiles (clothes and fabric), rice, and leather. Pakistan makes a lot of the world's sports equipment.
PAK.langs: Urdu and English are official. Plus Punjabi, Pashto, Sindhi, and many more.
PAK.gave: The number system we use today passed through Pakistani scholars, and polo was played here first.
PAK.bfast: Paratha (flaky flatbread) with fried eggs and sweet chai tea with cardamom.
PAK.big: About twice the size of California. Over 230 million people.

BHR.led: A king is in charge. The Al Khalifa family has ruled since the 1780s.
BHR.makes: Oil refining, aluminum, and banking. Bahrain was the first Gulf country to discover oil.
BHR.langs: Arabic. English is widely used in business.
BHR.gave: The first oil discovery on the Arabian side of the Gulf, the Tree of Life, and Formula 1 racing in the desert.
BHR.bfast: Balaleet (sweet vermicelli) with eggs, or bread with cheese and date syrup.
BHR.big: About 300 square miles. 33 islands in the Persian Gulf.

GRL.led: Greenland governs itself but Denmark handles defense and foreign affairs. An elected parliament runs local matters.
GRL.makes: Fish and shrimp. Fishing is the backbone of the economy. Mining is growing.
GRL.langs: Greenlandic (Kalaallisut) and Danish. Greenlandic is an Inuit language.
GRL.gave: The world's largest island, the Global Seed Vault's neighbor, and a frontline view of climate change.
GRL.bfast: Bread with butter and coffee, or dried fish and tea. Food has to be imported or hunted.
GRL.big: The world's largest island. About 836,000 square miles but only 56,000 people.

HKG.led: A Chief Executive is selected, but China's government has significant influence since the 2020 security law.
HKG.makes: Finance, trade, and shipping. Hong Kong is one of the world's biggest financial centers.
HKG.langs: Cantonese and English. Both are official. Mandarin is increasingly used.
HKG.gave: Dim sum culture, martial arts films (Bruce Lee, Jackie Chan), and one of the world's most spectacular skylines.
HKG.bfast: Congee with century egg, or a Hong Kong-style breakfast with toast, eggs, and milk tea.
HKG.big: About 426 square miles. 7.4 million people in a tiny space.

PSE.led: The Palestinian Authority governs parts of the West Bank. Hamas controls Gaza. The situation is complex and contested.
PSE.makes: Olive oil, stone, and textiles. The economy is heavily affected by the conflict.
PSE.langs: Arabic. English is taught in schools.
PSE.gave: Some of the oldest continuously inhabited cities, tatreez embroidery, and a cuisine (hummus, musakhan, knafeh) that has influenced the entire region.
PSE.bfast: Hummus with olive oil and bread, or ful medames with eggs and za'atar.
PSE.big: About 2,400 square miles total (West Bank and Gaza combined). About the size of Delaware.

ESH.led: Morocco controls most of the territory. The Sahrawi Arab Democratic Republic claims sovereignty. The UN considers it a non-self-governing territory.
ESH.makes: Phosphate mining and fishing. Morocco extracts resources from the territory.
ESH.langs: Arabic (Hassaniya dialect) and Spanish. French is also used.
ESH.gave: Sahrawi tea culture, desert survival knowledge, and one of the world's longest-running territorial disputes.
ESH.bfast: Bread with olive oil and tea, or couscous with milk. Desert-adapted food.
ESH.big: About 103,000 square miles. Mostly desert. Very few people.

GUF.led: Governed by France. French Guiana sends representatives to the French parliament.
GUF.makes: The European Space Agency's launch site is the main economic driver. Also gold mining and fishing.
GUF.langs: French. Creole and indigenous languages are also spoken.
GUF.gave: Europe's spaceport, incredible Amazonian biodiversity, and Devil's Island (the infamous prison colony).
GUF.bfast: Baguette with butter and coffee (French style), or bouillon with bread.
GUF.big: About 32,000 square miles. Over 90% is rainforest.

MYT.led: Governed by France as an overseas department since 2011. Sends representatives to the French parliament.
MYT.makes: Tourism, fishing, and agriculture. Ylang-ylang flowers for perfume.
MYT.langs: French and Shimaore (a Comorian language). Kibushi is also spoken.
MYT.gave: One of the world's largest enclosed lagoons and incredible marine biodiversity.
MYT.bfast: Mataba (cassava leaf stew) or bread with butter and coffee.
MYT.big: About 144 square miles. Two main islands.

GLP.led: Governed by France as an overseas department. Sends representatives to the French parliament.
GLP.gave: An active volcano (La Soufrière), butterfly-shaped islands, and Creole fusion cuisine.
GLP.bfast: Accras (salt cod fritters) with bread, or baguette with butter and coffee.
GLP.big: About 629 square miles. Two main islands shaped like a butterfly.

MTQ.led: Governed by France as an overseas department.
MTQ.gave: Some of the world's finest rum, the deadly 1902 eruption of Mount Pelée, and poet Aimé Césaire.
MTQ.bfast: Pain au beurre (buttered bread) with hot chocolate, or accras with bread.
MTQ.big: About 436 square miles. One volcanic island.

REU.led: Governed by France as an overseas department.
REU.makes: Sugar, vanilla, and rum. Tourism is growing fast.
REU.gave: One of the most active volcanoes on Earth (Piton de la Fournaise), premium vanilla, and incredible biodiversity.
REU.bfast: Baguette with butter and jam, or rougail saucisse (sausage in tomato sauce) leftovers.
REU.big: About 970 square miles. One volcanic island in the Indian Ocean.

PYF.led: A French overseas collectivity with significant local autonomy. An elected president runs local affairs.
PYF.makes: Tourism, black pearl farming, and fishing.
PYF.langs: French and Tahitian. Polynesian languages across the islands.
PYF.gave: Tahitian black pearls, Polynesian navigation and tattoo culture, and some of the most iconic tropical scenery on Earth.
PYF.bfast: Poisson cru (raw fish in coconut milk) or bread with butter and coffee.
PYF.big: 118 islands spread across an area the size of Western Europe, but only 1,500 square miles of land.

GUM.led: A US territory. An elected governor runs local affairs. Guamanians are US citizens but cannot vote for president.
GUM.makes: US military spending, tourism, and fishing.
GUM.langs: English and Chamorro. Both are official.
GUM.gave: 4,000 years of Chamorro culture, a strategic military position, and where America's day begins.
GUM.bfast: Red rice with eggs and longaniza (sausage), or pancit (noodles).
GUM.big: About 210 square miles. One island in the western Pacific.

ASM.led: A US territory. An elected governor runs local affairs. People born here are US nationals, NOT citizens.
ASM.makes: Tuna canning (StarKist), US military presence, and fishing.
ASM.langs: Samoan and English. Both are official.
ASM.gave: More NFL players per capita than anywhere on Earth, fa'a Samoa culture, and tuna for the entire US market.
ASM.bfast: Koko alaisa (cocoa rice) or bread with butter and tea.
ASM.big: About 77 square miles. Five main islands and two atolls.

MNP.led: A US commonwealth. An elected governor runs local affairs. Residents are US citizens.
MNP.makes: Tourism (mainly from Asia), garment manufacturing, and fishing.
MNP.langs: English, Chamorro, and Carolinian. All three are official.
MNP.gave: Major WWII battle sites, the deepest ocean nearby (Mariana Trench), and Chamorro and Carolinian cultures.
MNP.bfast: Rice with eggs and spam, or empanada.
MNP.big: About 179 square miles. 14 islands, only 3 are populated.

CUW.led: An autonomous country within the Kingdom of the Netherlands. An elected prime minister runs local affairs.
CUW.makes: Oil refining (one of the Caribbean's largest refineries), tourism, and banking.
CUW.langs: Papiamentu, Dutch, and English. Papiamentu is a Creole mixing Portuguese, Spanish, Dutch, and African languages.
CUW.gave: UNESCO-listed Willemstad waterfront, blue Curaçao liqueur, and Papiamentu (a unique language).
CUW.bfast: Funchi (cornmeal porridge) with cheese, or bread with peanut butter and cheese.

SXM.led: An autonomous country within the Kingdom of the Netherlands. An elected prime minister runs local affairs.
SXM.makes: Tourism and port services. Cruise ships are a major revenue source.
SXM.langs: English, Dutch, and Spanish. English is most common.
SXM.gave: The famous Maho Beach where planes fly over sunbathers, and the smallest island shared by two nations.
SXM.bfast: Johnny cake with saltfish, or bread with cheese and ham.
SXM.big: About 13 square miles. The Dutch side of a shared island.

BES.led: A special municipality of the Netherlands. Governed directly by the Dutch government.
BES.makes: Diving tourism, salt, and small-scale fishing.
BES.langs: Dutch and Papiamentu. English is widely spoken.
BES.gave: One of the world's best shore-diving destinations, protected reefs, and flamingo sanctuaries.
BES.bfast: Funchi with cheese, or bread with ham and Gouda.
BES.big: About 113 square miles. Three islands (Bonaire, Sint Eustatius, Saba).

AIA.led: A British Overseas Territory. A governor represents the King, and an elected Chief Minister runs local affairs.
AIA.makes: Tourism and offshore banking. Luxury tourism is the main industry.
AIA.gave: Some of the Caribbean's most beautiful beaches (Shoal Bay), luxury resorts, and a quiet island lifestyle.
AIA.bfast: Johnny cake with saltfish, or eggs with bread and tea.
AIA.big: About 35 square miles. One small flat island.

MSR.led: A British Overseas Territory. A governor represents the King.
MSR.makes: Very little since the volcano destroyed the economy. Aid and small-scale agriculture.
MSR.gave: A modern-day Pompeii (the buried capital of Plymouth), Irish Caribbean heritage, and incredible resilience.
MSR.bfast: Bread with cheese and tea, or green banana porridge.
MSR.big: About 40 square miles. Half is an exclusion zone around the volcano.

BLM.led: A French overseas collectivity. A president of the territorial council handles local affairs.
BLM.makes: Luxury tourism and duty-free shopping. One of the most exclusive destinations in the Caribbean.
BLM.langs: French. English is widely spoken in tourism.
BLM.gave: An exclusive celebrity vacation spot, French Caribbean cuisine, and the distinction of once being Swedish.
BLM.bfast: Croissant with butter and jam, or fresh tropical fruit with yogurt.
BLM.big: About 8 square miles. One tiny island.

MAF.led: A French overseas collectivity. An elected president of the territorial council runs local affairs.
MAF.makes: Tourism and restaurants. Known as the restaurant capital of the Caribbean.
MAF.gave: More restaurants per square mile than almost anywhere, a shared island with no border fence, and French Caribbean culture.
MAF.bfast: Croissant or baguette with coffee, or accras with bread.
MAF.big: About 21 square miles. The French side of a shared island.

SPM.led: A French overseas collectivity. Sends a senator and a deputy to the French parliament.
SPM.makes: Fishing and tourism. The economy is small and heavily subsidized by France.
SPM.gave: The last piece of France in North America, a unique Basque and Breton fishing culture, and persistent fog.
SPM.bfast: Baguette with butter and jam, and strong coffee. Fully French.
SPM.big: About 93 square miles. Two main islands off the coast of Canada.

ATF.led: Administered by France. No permanent residents, only rotating scientists and military personnel.
ATF.makes: Scientific research and fishing licenses. No commercial economy.
ATF.gave: Critical research on climate change, incredible wildlife (penguins, seals, albatrosses), and some of the most remote islands on Earth.
ATF.bfast: Whatever the research station serves. Typically French-influenced institutional meals.
ATF.big: About 3,000 square miles of scattered islands in the southern Indian Ocean.

SJM.led: Part of Norway. A governor represents the Norwegian government. Unique treaty allows citizens of many countries to live here.
SJM.makes: Research, tourism, and coal mining (declining). The seed vault is here.
SJM.langs: Norwegian. Many languages spoken due to the international community.
SJM.gave: The Global Seed Vault, polar bear encounters, and one of the most unique legal arrangements on Earth (open to citizens of 46 countries).
SJM.bfast: Bread with brunost (brown cheese) or kaviar, and strong coffee. Norwegian-style.
SJM.big: About 24,000 square miles. Mostly glaciers and mountains.

COK.led: Self-governing in free association with New Zealand. An elected prime minister runs the country. Cook Islanders are New Zealand citizens.
COK.makes: Tourism, fishing licenses, and black pearl farming.
COK.langs: English and Cook Islands Maori. Both are official.
COK.gave: Aitutaki lagoon (one of the most beautiful in the world), Polynesian dance culture, and a creative model of self-governance.
COK.bfast: Coconut bread with butter, or fried fish with rice.
COK.big: About 93 square miles of land spread across 15 islands in the South Pacific.

NIU.led: Self-governing in free association with New Zealand. An elected premier runs the country. Niueans are New Zealand citizens.
NIU.makes: Tourism, agriculture (taro, coconut), and government services.
NIU.langs: Niuean and English. Both are official.
NIU.gave: The first country with free nationwide WiFi, whale-watching from shore, and the nickname 'The Rock of Polynesia.'
NIU.bfast: Taro with coconut cream, or bread with butter and tea.
NIU.big: About 100 square miles. One raised coral island.

PCN.led: A British Overseas Territory. A governor (based in New Zealand) oversees affairs. An elected island mayor handles daily life.
PCN.makes: Honey (highly prized and pesticide-free), stamps, and handicrafts sold to passing ships.
PCN.langs: English and Pitkern (a Creole mixing 18th-century English with Tahitian).
PCN.gave: The Bounty mutiny story, the world's most remote inhabited community, and Pitkern language.
PCN.bfast: Pilhi (a baked banana dish) or bread with local honey.
PCN.big: About 18 square miles. One inhabited island. Population around 50.

WLF.led: A French overseas collectivity. A French administrator oversees the territory, but three traditional kings retain real authority.
WLF.makes: Agriculture (coconut, taro), fishing, and French government transfers.
WLF.gave: One of the last places where Polynesian monarchy and European governance coexist, and traditional kava ceremony culture.
WLF.bfast: Breadfruit with coconut cream, or baguette with butter and coffee.
WLF.big: About 54 square miles. Two main island groups.

CYN.led: A president and prime minister are elected. But only Turkey recognizes Northern Cyprus as a country. The rest of the world considers it part of Cyprus.
CYN.makes: Tourism, agriculture (citrus, olives), and university education (draws international students).
CYN.gave: Ancient Salamis ruins, Bellapais Abbey, uncrowded beaches, and the last divided capital city in the world (Nicosia).
CYN.bfast: Turkish-style: olives, tomatoes, cucumber, white cheese, bread, eggs, and tea.
CYN.big: About 1,300 square miles. The northern third of Cyprus island.
